<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
2: // License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
3: // file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.
4: 
5: /// Teste manual completo da biblioteca redmine-wrapper-rs.
6: ///
7: /// Avalia todos os 22 recursos da API em modo **somente leitura**:
8: /// nenhum dado é criado, alterado ou excluído.
9: ///
10: /// Uso:
11: /// ```bash
12: /// REDMINE_URL=https://redmine.seu-dominio.com REDMINE_TOKEN=sua-chave cargo run --example check
13: /// ```
14: ///
15: /// Cada operação é executada e seu resultado (PASS/FAIL/SKIP) é
16: /// registrado. Ao final, um resumo consolidado é exibido.
17: 
18: use std::env;
19: use std::fmt;
20: use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU32, Ordering};
21: 
22: use redmine_wrapper::core::config::RedmineConfigBuilder;
23: use redmine_wrapper::core::errors::RedmineError;
24: use redmine_wrapper::types::base::RedmineId;
25: use redmine_wrapper::types::issue::IssueFilter;
26: use redmine_wrapper::types::search::SearchFilter;
27: use redmine_wrapper::types::time_entry::TimeEntryFilter;
28: use redmine_wrapper::RedmineClient;
29: 
30: static PASS: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);
31: static FAIL: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);
32: static SKIP: AtomicU32 = AtomicU32::new(0);
33: 
34: fn ok(label: &str, detail: impl fmt::Display) {
35:     PASS.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
36:     println!("  \x1b[32mPASS\x1b[0m  {label}: {detail}");
37: }
38: 
39: fn fail(label: &str, detail: impl fmt::Display) {
40:     FAIL.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
41:     println!("  \x1b[31mFAIL\x1b[0m  {label}: {detail}");
42: }
43: 
44: fn skip(label: &str, detail: impl fmt::Display) {
45:     SKIP.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);
46:     println!("  \x1b[33mSKIP\x1b[0m  {label}: {detail}");
47: }
48: 
49: fn is_auth_err(e: &RedmineError) -> bool {
50:     matches!(e, RedmineError::Api { category, .. }
51:         if category.http_status() == 401 || category.http_status() == 403)
52: }
53: 
54: fn summary() {
55:     let p = PASS.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
56:     let f = FAIL.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
57:     let s = SKIP.load(Ordering::Relaxed);
58:     let total = p + f + s;
59:     println!();
60:     println!("═══════════════════════════════════════");
61:     println!("  RESULTADO FINAL");
62:     println!("  Total: {total:3}  PASS: {p:3}  FAIL: {f:3}  SKIP: {s:3}");
63:     println!("═══════════════════════════════════════");
64:     if f > 0 {
65:         std::process::exit(1);
66:     }
67: }
68: 
69: macro_rules! section {
70:     ($title:expr) => {
71:         println!();
72:         println!("─── {} ───", $title);
73:     };
74: }
75: 
76: fn main() {
77:     if env::var("RUST_LOG").is_ok() {
78:         env_logger::init();
79:     }
80: 
81:     let base_url = env::var("REDMINE_URL").expect("REDMINE_URL não definida");
82:     let token = env::var("REDMINE_TOKEN").ok();
83: 
84:     let config = RedmineConfigBuilder::default()
85:         .base_url(base_url.clone())
86:         .token(token.clone().unwrap_or_default())
87:         .build()
88:         .expect("falha ao construir config");
89:     let client = RedmineClient::new(config).expect("falha ao criar cliente");
90: 
91:     println!("Conectado a: {base_url}");
92:     if token.is_some() {
93:         println!("Autenticação: via token");
94:     } else {
95:         println!("Autenticação: anônima");
96:     }
97: 
98:     // ────────────────────────────────────────────
99:     // 1. my_account
100:     // ────────────────────────────────────────────
101:     section!("my_account");
102:     {
103:         match client.my_account.get() {
104:             Ok(a) => ok("my_account.get", format_args!("#{} {} {}", a.id, a.firstname.as_deref().unwrap_or("?"), a.lastname.as_deref().unwrap_or("?"))),
105:             Err(RedmineError::Api { ref category, .. }) if category.http_status() == 403 => skip("my_account.get", "sem permissão (403) — token pode ser anônimo"),
106:             Err(e) => fail("my_account.get", e),
107:         }
108:     }
109: 
110:     // ────────────────────────────────────────────
111:     // 2. projects
112:     // ────────────────────────────────────────────
113:     section!("projects");
114:     {
115:         match client.projects.list() {
116:             Ok(list) => {
117:                 ok("projects.list", format_args!("{} projeto(s)", list.len()));
118:                 if let Some(p) = list.first() {
119:                     let pid = p.id;
120:                     match client.projects.get(pid) {
121:                         Ok(p2) => ok("projects.get", format_args!("#{} {}", p2.id, p2.name.as_deref().unwrap_or("?"))),
122:                         Err(e) => fail("projects.get", e),
123:                     }
124:                     match client.projects.get_with_includes(pid, &["trackers", "issue_categories"]) {
125:                         Ok(p2) => ok("projects.get_with_includes", format_args!("#{} com includes", p2.id)),
126:                         Err(e) => fail("projects.get_with_includes", e),
127:                     }
128:                 } else {
129:                     skip("projects.get", "nenhum projeto disponível");
130:                     skip("projects.get_with_includes", "nenhum projeto disponível");
131:                 }
132:             }
133:             Err(RedmineError::Api { ref category, .. }) if category.http_status() == 403 => skip("projects.list", "sem permissão (403)"),
134:             Err(e) => fail("projects.list", e),
135:         }
136:     }
137: 
138:     // ────────────────────────────────────────────
139:     // 3. users
140:     // ────────────────────────────────────────────
141:     section!("users");
142:     {
143:         match client.users.get_current() {
144:             Ok(u) => ok("users.get_current", format_args!("#{} {} {}", u.id, u.firstname.as_deref().unwrap_or("?"), u.lastname.as_deref().unwrap_or("?"))),
145:             Err(RedmineError::Api { ref category, .. }) if category.http_status() == 403 => skip("users.get_current", "sem permissão (403)"),
146:             Err(e) => fail("users.get_current", e),
147:         }
148:         match client.users.list(None) {
149:             Ok(list) => {
150:                 ok("users.list", format_args!("{} usuário(s)", list.len()));
151:                 if let Some(u) = list.first() {
152:                     let uid = u.id;
153:                     match client.users.get(uid) {
154:                         Ok(u2) => ok("users.get", format_args!("#{} {}", u2.id, u2.login.as_deref().unwrap_or("?"))),
155:                         Err(e) => fail("users.get", e),
156:                     }
157:                     match client.users.get_with_includes(uid, &["memberships", "groups"]) {
158:                         Ok(u2) => ok("users.get_with_includes", format_args!("#{} com includes", u2.id)),
159:                         Err(e) => fail("users.get_with_includes", e),
160:                     }
161:                 } else {
162:                     skip("users.get", "nenhum usuário disponível");

173:     // ────────────────────────────────────────────
174:     // 4. issues
175:     // ────────────────────────────────────────────
176:     section!("issues");
177:     {
178:         match client.issues.list(None) {

183:                     first_issue_id = Some(iid);

188:                     match client.issues.get_with_includes(iid, &["journals", "attachments", "relations"]) {

190:                         Err(e) => fail("issues.get_with_includes", e),
191:                     }

194:                         Err(e) => fail("issues.get_allowed_statuses", e),
195:                     }

199:                     skip("issues.get_allowed_statuses", "nenhuma issue disponível");
200:                 }
201:             }

203:             Err(e) => fail("issues.list", e),
204:         }

206:         let filter = IssueFilter {
207:             status_id: Some("open".into()),
208:             ..Default::default()
209:         };

11: /// Configura um mock server e retorna o server + cliente configurado.
12: fn setup() -> (MockServer, RedmineClient) {
13:     let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();
14:     let server = rt.block_on(MockServer::start());
15: 
16:     let config = RedmineConfig {
17:         base_url: server.uri(),
18:         token: Some("test-token".into()),
19:         ..Default::default()
20:     };
21: 
22:     let client = RedmineClient::new(config).unwrap();
23:     (server, client)
24: }
25: 
26: #[test]
27: fn test_client_creation() {
28:     let config = RedmineConfig {
29:         base_url: "https://redmine.example.com".into(),
30:         token: Some("token".into()),
31:         ..Default::default()
32:     };
33:     let client = RedmineClient::new(config).unwrap();
34:     assert_eq!(client.config.base_url, "https://redmine.example.com");
35: }
36: 
37: #[test]
38: fn test_client_creation_fails_without_url() {
39:     let config = RedmineConfig {
40:         base_url: "".into(),
41:         token: Some("token".into()),
42:         ..Default::default()
43:     };
44:     let result = RedmineClient::new(config);
45:     assert!(result.is_err());
46: }
47: 
48: #[test]
49: fn test_client_creation_succeeds_without_token() {
50:     let config = RedmineConfig {
51:         base_url: "https://redmine.example.com".into(),
52:         token: None,
53:         ..Default::default()
54:     };
55:     let client = RedmineClient::new(config).unwrap();
56:     assert!(client.config.token.is_none());
57: }
58: 
59: #[test]
60: fn test_auth_header_sent() {
61:     let (server, client) = setup();
62: 
63:     let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();
64:     rt.block_on(async {
65:         Mock::given(method("GET"))
66:             .and(path("/issues.json"))
67:             .and(header("X-Redmine-API-Key", "test-token"))
68:             .respond_with(
69:                 ResponseTemplate::new(200)
70:                     .set_body_string(r#"{"issues":[],"total_count":0,"limit":25,"offset":0}"#),
71:             )
72:             .mount(&server)
73:             .await;
74:     });
75: 
76:     let result = client.issues.list(None);
77:     assert!(result.is_ok());
78: }
79: 
80: #[test]
81: fn test_issues_list() {
82:     let (server, client) = setup();
83: 
84:     let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();
85:     rt.block_on(async {
86:         Mock::given(method("GET"))
87:             .and(path("/issues.json"))
88:             .respond_with(
89:                 ResponseTemplate::new(200).set_body_string(
90:                     r#"{"issues":[{"id":1,"subject":"Test Issue"}],"total_count":1,"limit":25,"offset":0}"#,
91:                 ),
92:             )
93:             .mount(&server)
94:             .await;
95:     });
96: 
97:     let result = client.issues.list(None).unwrap();
98:     assert_eq!(result.len(), 1);
99:     assert_eq!(result[0].id, 1);
100: }
101: 
102: #[test]
103: fn test_issues_get() {
104:     let (server, client) = setup();
105: 
106:     let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();
107:     rt.block_on(async {
108:         Mock::given(method("GET"))
109:             .and(path("/issues/1.json"))
110:             .respond_with(
111:                 ResponseTemplate::new(200).set_body_string(
112:                     r#"{"issue":{"id":1,"subject":"Test","project":{"id":1,"name":"P1"}}}"#,
113:                 ),
114:             )
115:             .mount(&server)
116:             .await;
117:     });
118: 
119:     let result = client.issues.get(1).unwrap();
120:     assert_eq!(result.id, 1);
121:     assert_eq!(result.subject.unwrap(), "Test");
122: }
123: 
124: #[test]
125: fn test_404_error() {
126:     let (server, client) = setup();
127: 
128:     let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();
129:     rt.block_on(async {
130:         Mock::given(method("GET"))
131:             .and(path("/issues/999.json"))
132:             .respond_with(ResponseTemplate::new(404))
133:             .mount(&server)
134:             .await;
135:     });
136: 
137:     let result = client.issues.get(999);
138:     assert!(result.is_err());
139:     let err = result.unwrap_err();
140:     assert_eq!(err.category(), Some(ErrorCategory::ResourceNotFound));
141: }
142: 
143: #[test]
144: fn test_401_error() {
145:     let (server, client) = setup();
146: 
147:     let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();
148:     rt.block_on(async {
149:         Mock::given(method("GET"))
150:             .and(path("/issues.json"))
151:             .respond_with(ResponseTemplate::new(401))
152:             .mount(&server)
153:             .await;
154:     });
155: 
156:     let result = client.issues.list(None);
157:     assert!(result.is_err());
158:     let err = result.unwrap_err();
159:     assert_eq!(err.category(), Some(ErrorCategory::AuthenticationFailed));
160: }
161: 
162: #[test]
163: fn test_validation_error() {
164:     let (server, client) = setup();
165: 
166:     let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();
167:     rt.block_on(async {
168:         Mock::given(method("POST"))
169:             .and(path("/issues.json"))
170:             .respond_with(
171:                 ResponseTemplate::new(422)
172:                     .set_body_string(r#"{"errors":["Subject não pode ficar em branco"]}"#),
173:             )
174:             .mount(&server)
175:             .await;
176:     });
177: 
178:     let payload = redmine_wrapper::types::issue::CreateIssuePayload {
179:         project_id: 1,
180:         subject: "".into(),
181:         tracker_id: None,
182:         status_id: None,
183:         priority_id: None,
184:         description: None,
185:         category_id: None,
186:         assigned_to_id: None,
187:         parent_issue_id: None,
188:         fixed_version_id: None,
189:         estimated_hours: None,
190:         done_ratio: None,
191:         is_private: None,
192:         custom_fields: None,
193:         uploads: None,
194:         watcher_user_ids: None,
195:     };
196: 
197:     let result = client.issues.create(&payload);
198:     assert!(result.is_err());
199:     let err = result.unwrap_err();
200:     assert_eq!(err.category(), Some(ErrorCategory::ValidationError));
201: }
202: 
203: #[test]
204: fn test_projects_list() {
205:     let (server, client) = setup();
206: 
207:     let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();
208:     rt.block_on(async {
209:         Mock::given(method("GET"))
210:             .and(path("/projects.json"))
211:             .respond_with(
212:                 ResponseTemplate::new(200).set_body_string(
213:                     r#"{"projects":[{"id":1,"name":"Project 1","identifier":"proj1"}],"total_count":1,"limit":25,"offset":0}"#,
214:                 ),
215:             )
216:             .mount(&server)
217:             .await;
218:     });
219: 
220:     let result = client.projects.list().unwrap();
221:     assert_eq!(result.len(), 1);
222:     assert_eq!(result[0].name.as_deref(), Some("Project 1"));
223: }
224: 
225: #[test]
226: fn test_auth_method_default() {
227:     let config = RedmineConfig {
228:         base_url: "https://redmine.example.com".into(),
229:         token: Some("test".into()),
230:         ..Default::default()
231:     };
232:     let client = RedmineClient::new(config).unwrap();
233:     assert_eq!(client.config.auth_method, redmine_wrapper::AuthMethod::Header);
234: }

<content>
1: # 7 — Generics, Traits e Tipos Avançados
2: 
3: ## Generics
4: 
5: ### Em funções
6: 
7: ```rust
8: fn largest<T: PartialOrd>(list: &[T]) -> &T {
9:     let mut largest = &list[0];
10:     for item in list {
11:         if item > largest { largest = item; }
12:     }
13:     largest
14: }
15: ```
16: 
17: ### Em structs
18: 
19: ```rust
20: struct Point<T, U> {
21:     x: T,
22:     y: U,
23: }
24: 
25: // Métodos genéricos
26: impl<T: Clone, U: Clone> Point<T, U> {
27:     fn mixup<V, W>(self, other: Point<V, W>) -> Point<T, W> {
28:         Point { x: self.x, y: other.y }
29:     }
30: }
31: ```
32: 
33: ### Em enums
34: 
35: ```rust
36: enum Option<T> { Some(T), None }
37: enum Result<T, E> { Ok(T), Err(E) }
38: ```
39: 
40: ### Monomorfização
41: 
42: Generics são **zero-cost**: o compilador gera código específico para
43: cada tipo concreto (monomorfização). Não há virtual dispatch.
44: 
45: ## Traits
46: 
47: ### Definição e implementação
48: 
49: ```rust
50: trait Summary {
51:     fn summarize(&self) -> String;
52:     fn author(&self) -> String {
53:         String::from("unknown")  // default implementation
54:     }
55:     fn summarize_with_author(&self) -> String {
56:         format!("by {}: {}", self.author(), self.summarize())
57:     }
58: }
59: 
60: impl Summary for NewsArticle {
61:     fn summarize(&self) -> String {
62:         format!("{}, by {}", self.headline, self.author)
63:     }
64: }
65: ```
66: 
67: ### Trait Bounds
68: 
69: ```rust
70: // Sintaxe básica
71: fn notify<T: Summary>(item: &T) {}
72: 
73: // Múltiplos bounds
74: fn notify<T: Summary + Display>(item: &T) {}
75: 
76: // where clause (mais legível com muitos bounds)
77: fn some_function<T, U>(t: &T, u: &U) -> i32
78: where
79:     T: Display + Clone,
80:     U: Clone + Debug,
81: { 42 }
82: 
83: // impl Trait syntax (argumento)
84: fn print_summary(item: &impl Summary) {}
85: // equivalente a generic com trait bound
86: 
87: // impl Trait syntax (retorno)
88: fn returns_summarizable() -> impl Summary {
89:     Tweet { ... }
90: }
91: ```
92: 
93: ### Blanket Implementations
94: 
95: ```rust
96: impl<T: Display> ToString for T {
97:     // Tudo que implementa Display ganha to_string() automaticamente
98: }
99: ```
100: 
101: ### Supertraits
102: 
103: ```rust
104: trait OutlinePrint: Display {
105:     fn outline_print(&self) {
106:         let output = self.to_string();
107:         println!("{output}");
108:     }
109: }
110: // OutlinePrint só pode ser implementado para tipos que implementam Display
111: ```
112: 
113: ### Fully Qualified Syntax
114: 
115: ```rust
116: trait Animal { fn name(&self) -> String; }
117: trait Pet { fn name(&self) -> String; }
118: 
119: struct Dog;
120: impl Animal for Dog { fn name(&self) -> String { "Dog".into() } }
121: impl Pet for Dog { fn name(&self) -> String { "Rex".into() } }
122: 
123: fn main() {
124:     let dog = Dog;
125:     println!("{}", <Dog as Animal>::name(&dog));
126:     println!("{}", <Dog as Pet>::name(&dog));
127: }
128: ```
129: 
130: ### Marker Traits
131: 
132: ```rust
133: // Traits sem métodos — funcionam como marcas no type system
134: trait Send: unsafe impl {}    // seguro para enviar entre threads
135: trait Sync: unsafe impl {}    // seguro para compartilhar entre threads
136: trait Copy: Clone {}          // bitwise copy é seguro
137: trait Sized {}                // tamanho conhecido em compile-time
138: ```
139: 
140: ## Associated Types
141: 
142: ```rust
143: trait Iterator {
144:     type Item;                               // associated type
145:     fn next(&mut self) -> Option<Self::Item>;
146: }
147: 
148: impl Iterator for Counter {
149:     type Item = u32;                         // definido na impl
150:     fn next(&mut self) -> Option<Self::Item> { todo!() }
151: }
152: ```
153: 
154: ### Associated Constants
155: 
156: ```rust
157: trait ConstValue {
158:     const DEFAULT: Self;
159:     fn get(&self) -> u32;
160: }
161: 
162: impl ConstValue for u32 {
163:     const DEFAULT: u32 = 0;
164:     fn get(&self) -> u32 { *self }
165: }
166: ```
167: 
168: ## Advanced Traits
169: 
170: ### Default Generic Parameters (Operator Overloading)
171: 
172: ```rust
173: use std::ops::Add;
174: 
175: #[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone, PartialEq)]
176: struct Point {
177:     x: i32,
178:     y: i32,
179: }
180: 
181: impl Add for Point {
182:     type Output = Point;
183:     fn add(self, other: Point) -> Point {
184:         Point { x: self.x + other.x, y: self.y + other.y }
185:     }
186: }
187: 
188: // Com parâmetro genérico default
189: trait Add<Rhs = Self> {
190:     type Output;
191:     fn add(self, rhs: Rhs) -> Self::Output;
192: }
193: 
194: // Milimetros + Metros
195: struct Millimetres(u32);
196: struct Metres(u32);
197: impl Add<Metres> for Millimetres {
198:     type Output = Millimetres;
199:     fn add(self, other: Metres) -> Millimetres {
200:         Millimetres(self.0 + (other.0 * 1000))
201:     }
202: }
203: ```
204: 
205: ### Newtype Pattern
206: 
207: ```rust
208: // Wrapper para implementar trait externa em tipo externo (orphan rule)
209: struct Wrapper(Vec<String>);
210: 
211: impl Display for Wrapper {
212:     fn fmt(&self, f: &mut Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
213:         write!(f, "[{}]", self.0.join(", "))
214:     }
215: }
216: 
217: // Type safety
218: struct Email(String);
219: struct Phone(String);
220: fn send_email(to: Email) {}
221: 
222: send_email(Email("alice@example.com".into()));
223: // send_email(Phone("123".into())); // ERROR!
224: ```
225: 
226: ## Type Aliases
227: 
228: ```rust
229: type Kilometers = i32;           // sinônimo
230: type Thunk = Box<dyn Fn() + Send + 'static>;
231: type Result<T> = std::result::Result<T, std::io::Error>;
232: 
233: // Uso em bibliotecas
234: pub type IoResult<T> = Result<T, io::Error>;
235: ```
236: 
237: ## Never Type (!)
238: 
239: ```rust
240: // ! = never type — "nunca retorna"
241: fn forever() -> ! {
242:     loop { println!("forever"); }
243: }
244: 
245: // Útil em match com arms de tipos diferentes
246: let guess: u32 = match guess.trim().parse() {
247:     Ok(num) => num,
248:     Err(_) => continue,  // continue é !
249: };
250: 
251: // panic! e loop infinito também são !
252: ```
253: 
254: ## Dynamically Sized Types (DSTs)
255: 
256: ```rust
257: // DSTs: tamanho não conhecido em compile-time
258: // str, [T], dyn Trait
259: 
260: // Não podem ser armazenados diretamente na stack:
261: // let s: str = "hello";      // ERROR: não Sized
262: // let arr: [u32] = [1, 2];   // ERROR
263: 
264: // Sempre atrás de um ponteiro:
265: let s: &str = "hello";              // fat pointer (ptr + len)
266: let arr: &[i32] = &[1, 2, 3];      // fat pointer (ptr + len)
267: let obj: &dyn Display = &some_val; // fat pointer (ptr + vtable)
268: 
269: // ?Sized — trait bound que permite DST ou Sized
270: fn generic<T: ?Sized>(t: &T) {}
271: ```
272: 
273: ## PhantomData
274: 
275: ```rust
276: use std::marker::PhantomData;
277: 
278: // Marca que um tipo possui parâmetro genérico mesmo sem campo
279: struct Iter<'a, T: 'a> {
280:     ptr: *const T,
281:     _marker: PhantomData<&'a T>, // ownership-like semantics
282: }
283: 
284: // Unidades de medida via phantom types
285: struct Meter<Unit>(f64, PhantomData<Unit>);
286: struct Cm;
287: struct Km;
288: 
289: let dist = Meter::<Km>(5.0, PhantomData);
290: ```
291: 
292: ## Coherence (Orphan Rule)
293: 
294: - Você pode implementar **seu trait** em **qualquer tipo**
295: - Você pode implementar **qualquer trait** em **seu tipo**
296: - Você **não pode** implementar trait externo em tipo externo
297: 
298: ```rust
299: // Seu trait, tipo externo → OK
300: trait MyTrait {}
301: impl MyTrait for Vec<String> {}
302: 
303: // Trait externo, seu tipo → OK
304: impl Display for MyStruct {}
305: 
306: // Trait externo, tipo externo → ERROR
307: // impl Display for Vec<String> {} // ERROR!
308: ```
309: 
310: ## Boas Práticas
311: 
312: 1. **Preferir `impl Trait`** em argumentos para funções simples
313: 2. **Usar `where` clauses** quando múltiplos bounds ficam ilegíveis
314: 3. **Newtype pattern** para type safety e implementação de traits
315:    externas
316: 4. **Evitar trait objects** (`dyn Trait`) onde generics bastam
317: 5. **Use marker traits** para adicionar garantias no type system
318: 6. **Associated types** para traits com uma única output type
319: 7. **Default generic params** para operadores e extensibilidade
320: 8. **PhantomData** para parâmetros genéricos que só existem no
321:    type level

(End of file - total 321 lines)
</content>
```

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # 13 — Testes
2: 
3: ## Tipos de Teste
4: 
5: | Tipo | Localização | Propósito |
6: |------|-------------|-----------|
7: | Unitários | `src/` (inline) | Testar unidades individuais |
8: | Integração | `tests/` | Testar API pública |
9: | Doc Tests | Doc comments | Exemplos executáveis |
10: | Benchmarks | `benches/` | Performance |
11: 
12: ## Testes Unitários
13: 
14: ```rust
15: // src/lib.rs ou no módulo específico
16: 
17: pub fn add(left: u64, right: u64) -> u64 {
18:     left + right
19: }
20: 
21: #[cfg(test)]
22: mod tests {
23:     use super::*;
24: 
25:     #[test]
26:     fn it_works() {
27:         let result = add(2, 2);
28:         assert_eq!(result, 4);
29:     }
30: 
31:     #[test]
32:     fn with_result() -> Result<(), String> {
33:         if 2 + 2 == 4 { Ok(()) } else { Err("wrong".into()) }
34:     }
35: 
36:     #[test]
37:     #[should_panic(expected = "overflow")]
38:     fn panics() {
39:         add(u64::MAX, 1);
40:     }
41: 
42:     #[test]
43:     #[ignore = "expensive"]
44:     fn expensive_test() {
45:         // só roda com cargo test -- --ignored
46:     }
47: }
48: ```
49: 
50: ### Macros de Asserção
51: 
52: ```rust
53: assert!(condition);
54: assert!(condition, "custom message: {val}");
55: 
56: assert_eq!(a, b);       // a == b (PartialEq + Debug)
57: assert_ne!(a, b);       // a != b
58: 
59: // Custom failure message
60: assert_eq!(left, right, "left: {left}, right: {right}");
61: 
62: // Para tipos sem PartialEq
63: assert!(a == b, "{a:?} != {b:?}");
64: ```
65: 
66: ## Testes de Integração
67: 
68: ```rust
69: // tests/integration_test.rs
70: use my_crate;  // tratado como crate externo
71: 
72: #[test]
73: fn test_add() {
74:     assert_eq!(my_crate::add(2, 2), 4);
75: }
76: 
77: // tests/common/mod.rs — módulo compartilhado
78: // tests/common/mod.rs (não compila como test separado)
79: pub fn setup() -> Config {
80:     Config { /* ... */ }
81: }
82: ```
83: 
84: ```bash
85: cargo test                    # todos os testes
86: cargo test --test integration_test  # só integração
87: cargo test nome_parcial       # filtra por nome
88: cargo test -- --test-threads=1
89: cargo test -- --show-output
90: ```
91: 
92: ## Doc Tests
93: 
94: ```rust
95: /// Adiciona dois números.
96: ///
97: /// # Examples
98: ///
99: /// ```
100: /// use my_crate::add;
101: /// assert_eq!(add(2, 3), 5);
102: /// ```
103: ///
104: /// ```rust,should_panic
105: /// // este teste deve panicar
106: /// ```
107: ///
108: /// ```rust,ignore
109: /// // não compila como teste
110: /// ```
111: ///
112: /// ```rust,no_run
113: /// // compila mas não executa
114: /// ```
115: pub fn add(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32 { a + b }
116: ```
117: 
118: ```bash
119: cargo test --doc              # só doc tests
120: cargo test                    # doc tests + unitários + integração
121: ```
122: 
123: ## Benchmarks
124: 
125: ### Standard (nightly)
126: 
127: ```rust
128: #![feature(test)]
129: extern crate test;
130: use test::Bencher;
131: 
132: #[bench]
133: fn bench_add(b: &mut Bencher) {
134:     b.iter(|| add(1, 2));
135: }
136: ```
137: 
138: ### Criterion (stable — recomendado)
139: 
140: ```toml
141: [dev-dependencies]
142: criterion = { version = "0.5", features = ["html_reports"] }
143: 
144: [[bench]]
145: name = "my_bench"
146: harness = false
147: ```
148: 
149: ```rust
150: // benches/my_bench.rs
151: use criterion::{black_box, criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion};
152: 
153: fn fibonacci(n: u64) -> u64 {
154:     match n { 0 => 1, 1 => 1, n => fibonacci(n-1) + fibonacci(n-2) }
155: }
156: 
157: fn bench_fib(c: &mut Criterion) {
158:     c.bench_function("fib 20", |b| b.iter(|| fibonacci(black_box(20))));
159: }
160: 
161: criterion_group!(benches, bench_fib);
162: criterion_main!(benches);
163: ```
164: 
165: ```bash
166: cargo bench
167: ```
168: 
169: ## Test Organization
170: 
171: ```rust
172: // src/lib.rs
173: pub fn public_api() {}
174: fn private_helpers() {}
175: 
176: #[cfg(test)]
177: mod tests {
178:     use super::*;  // importa items privados também
179: 
180:     #[test]
181:     fn test_private_helper() {
182:         private_helpers();  // privado, mas acessível aqui
183:     }
184: }
185: ```
186: 
187: ### Mocking
188: 
189: ```rust
190: // Sem framework
191: trait DataSource {
192:     fn fetch(&self) -> Vec<i32>;
193: }
194: 
195: struct RealDB;
196: impl DataSource for RealDB { /* ... */ }
197: 
198: #[cfg(test)]
199: struct MockDB;
200: impl DataSource for MockDB {
201:     fn fetch(&self) -> Vec<i32> { vec![1, 2, 3] }
202: }
203: 
204: #[cfg(test)]
205: mod tests {
206:     fn test_with_mock() {
207:         let mock = MockDB;
208:         let result = process(mock);
209:         assert_eq!(result, 6);
210:     }
211: }
212: 
213: // mockall crate (auto-generate mocks)
214: use mockall::automock;
215: 
216: #[automock]
217: trait DataSource {
218:     fn fetch(&self) -> Vec<i32>;
219: }
220: ```
221: 
222: ## Usando `cargo test` Avançado
223: 
224: ```bash
225: cargo test              # todos os testes
226: cargo test test_name    # filtra por nome
227: cargo test --lib        # só testes unitários
228: cargo test --test integ # só integração em tests/integ.rs
229: cargo test -p my_crate  # workspace: só um crate
230: 
231: # Flags
232: -- --test-threads=1     # serial
233: -- --nocapture          # mostra stdout
234: -- --show-output        # mostra output de testes
235: -- --ignored            # só testes com #[ignore]
236: -- --include-ignored    # todos (incluindo ignored)
237: ```
238: 
239: ## Para onde vão os testes
240: 
241: ```
242: src/
243: ├── lib.rs              # código principal
244: └── math.rs             # código principal
245:     └── ...             # #[cfg(test)] mod tests inline
246: tests/
247: ├── integration.rs
248: └── common/
249:     └── mod.rs          # módulo compartilhado
250: benches/
251: └── my_bench.rs
252: ```
253: 
254: ## Boas Práticas
255: 
256: 1. **Testes unitários no mesmo arquivo** — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`
257: 2. **`#[should_panic]`** para testar condições de erro
258: 3. **`Result<(), E>`** em testes — mais idiomático que `assert!`
259: 4. **Doc tests são testes de verdade** — mantenha-os atualizados
260: 5. **Testes de integração em `tests/`** — testam API pública
261: 6. **Mock traits** para isolar testes de I/O
262: 7. **`cargo nextest`** é mais rápido que `cargo test` para CI
263: 8. **Teste nomes descritivos** — `test_parse_empty_string` em vez de
264:    `test1`
265: 9. **Property-based testing com `proptest`** — testa casos aleatórios
266: 10. **Code coverage com `cargo tarpaulin`** ou `cargo-llvm-cov`

(End of file - total 266 lines)
</content>
```

<content>
1: # 15 — Cargo em Profundidade
2: 
3: ## Cargo.toml Completo
4: 
5: ```toml
6: [package]
7: name = "my_app"
8: version = "0.1.0"
9: edition = "2024"
10: rust-version = "1.85"
11: authors = ["Alice <alice@example.com>"]
12: description = "A brief description"
13: license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
14: license-file = "LICENSE"
15: readme = "README.md"
16: homepage = "https://example.com"
17: repository = "https://github.com/user/repo"
18: documentation = "https://docs.rs/my_app"
19: keywords = ["cli", "tool"]
20: categories = ["command-line-utilities"]
21: publish = false   # impede publish acidental
22: resolver = "2"
23: default-run = "my_app"  # qual binário rodar com cargo run
24: build = "build.rs"
25: links = "my_native_lib"
26: exclude = ["ci/", "*.png"]
27: include = ["src/**/*", "Cargo.toml"]
28: ```
29: 
30: ### Targets
31: 
32: ```toml
33: [lib]
34: name = "my_lib"
35: path = "src/lib.rs"
36: crate-type = ["lib", "cdylib"]  # lib + shared library
37: doc = true
38: doctest = true
39: bench = true
40: test = true
41: 
42: [[bin]]
43: name = "cli"
44: path = "src/bin/cli.rs"
45: required-features = ["cli"]
46: 
47: [[example]]
48: name = "example1"
49: path = "examples/example1.rs"
50: 
51: [[test]]
52: name = "integration"
53: path = "tests/integration.rs"
54: 
55: [[bench]]
56: name = "benchmark"
57: harness = false  # desativa harness padrão
58: ```
59: 
60: ## Dependencies
61: 
62: ```toml
63: [dependencies]
64: # Crates.io (semver caret por default)
65: serde = "1"                         # ^1.0.0
66: serde = "1.2"                       # ^1.2.0
67: serde = "=1.2.3"                    # exato
68: serde = "~1.2"                      # >=1.2.0, <1.3.0
69: serde = ">=1.0, <2.0"              # range
70: 
71: # Git
72: tokio = { git = "https://github.com/tokio-rs/tokio", branch = "master" }
73: my_crate = { git = "https://github.com/user/repo", rev = "abc123" }
74: 
75: # Path
76: local_dep = { path = "../local_dep" }
77: 
78: # Multiple sources (dev usa path, publish usa version)
79: my_crate = { path = "../my_crate", version = "0.1" }
80: 
81: # Platform-specific
82: [target.'cfg(windows)'.dependencies]
83: winapi = "0.3"
84: 
85: [target.'cfg(target_arch = "x86_64")'.dependencies]
86: special = "0.1"
87: 
88: # Renaming (package name different from dependency key)
89: serde_json = { package = "serde_json_alt", version = "1" }
90: 
91: # Optional
92: tokio = { version = "1", optional = true }
93: 
94: # Dev-only
95: [dev-dependencies]
96: criterion = "0.5"
97: 
98: # Build-only
99: [build-dependencies]
100: cc = "1"
101: ```
102: 
103: ### Resolver
104: 
105: ```toml
106: [package]
107: resolver = "2"  # Edition 2021+, recomendado
108: ```
109: 
110: | Resolver | Comportamento |
111: |----------|---------------|
112: | 1 (2015) | Unifica features entre targets |
113: | 2 (2021) | Features separados por target, build-deps não unificam |
114: | 3 (nightly) | MSRV-aware |
115: 
116: ## Profiles
117: 
118: ```toml
119: [profile.dev]
120: opt-level = 0           # sem otimização
121: debug = true            # símbolos completos
122: debug-assertions = true
123: overflow-checks = true
124: incremental = true      # compilação incremental
125: codegen-units = 256     # mais unidades = compilação mais rápida
126: lto = false
127: panic = "unwind"
128: 
129: [profile.release]
130: opt-level = 3           # -O3
131: debug = false
132: debug-assertions = false
133: overflow-checks = false
134: lto = "fat"             # link-time optimization
135: codegen-units = 1       # melhor otimização
136: strip = "symbols"       # remove símbolos
137: panic = "abort"         # binário menor (sem unwinding)
138: incremental = false
139: 
140: [profile.bench]
141: inherits = "release"    # herda de release
142: debug = 1               # line tables only
143: 
144: # Override por pacote
145: [profile.dev.package.'*']
146: opt-level = 2           # otimiza todas deps em dev
147: 
148: [profile.dev.package.serde]
149: opt-level = 3
150: 
151: # Build override (para build scripts)
152: [profile.dev.build-override]
153: opt-level = 3
154: ```
155: 
156: ## Workspaces
157: 
158: ```toml
159: # Cargo.toml (root)
160: [workspace]
161: members = [
162:     "crates/*",
163:     "tools/cli",
164: ]
165: exclude = ["legacy"]
166: resolver = "2"
167: default-members = ["crates/app", "tools/cli"]
168: 
169: # Shared metadata
170: [workspace.package]
171: version = "0.1.0"
172: edition = "2024"
173: authors = ["Alice <alice@example.com>"]
174: 
175: [workspace.dependencies]
176: serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
177: tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
178: thiserror = "2"
179: 
180: [workspace.lints]
181: rust.unsafe_code = "forbid"
182: ```
183: 
184: ```toml
185: # crates/app/Cargo.toml
186: [package]
187: name = "app"
188: version.workspace = true
189: edition.workspace = true
190: 
191: [dependencies]
192: serde.workspace = true
193: tokio.workspace = true
194: my_utils = { path = "../utils" }
195: ```
196: 
197: ## Features
198: 
199: ```toml
200: [features]
201: default = ["std"]
202: std = []
203: serde = ["dep:serde"]
204: derive = []
205: full = ["std", "serde", "derive", "async"]
206: 
207: # Conditional dependencies
208: [dependencies]
209: serde = { version = "1", optional = true, features = ["derive"] }
210: tokio = { version = "1", optional = true, default-features = false }
211: 
212: # Weak dependencies (Edition 2021+)
213: # "rgb?/serde" = depende de rgb, mas não ativa rgb
214: ```
215: 
216: ```rust
217: // Feature gates
218: #[cfg(feature = "serde")]
219: impl Serialize for MyType {}
220: 
221: #[cfg(not(feature = "std"))]
222: extern crate alloc;
223: ```
224: 
225: ## Build Scripts
226: 
227: ```rust
228: // build.rs
229: fn main() {
230:     // Gera cfg condicional
231:     println!("cargo::rustc-cfg=has_foo");
232: 
233:     // Adiciona link search path
234:     println!("cargo::rustc-link-search=/path/to/libs");
235: 
236:     // Link library
237:     println!("cargo::rustc-link-lib=static=foo");
238:     println!("cargo::rustc-link-arg=-Wl,-rpath,/path/to");
239: 
240:     // Re-run conditions
241:     println!("cargo::rerun-if-changed=src/ffi/wrapper.h");
242:     println!("cargo::rerun-if-env-changed=LLVM_CONFIG_PATH");
243: 
244:     // Link args
245:     println!("cargo::rustc-link-arg-bins=-static");
246:     println!("cargo::rustc-link-arg-tests=-lm");
247: 
248:     // Erros (build falha)
249:     println!("cargo::error=missing dependency: foo");
250: 
251:     // Generate code
252:     let out_dir = std::env::var("OUT_DIR").unwrap();
253:     std::fs::write(format!("{out_dir}/generated.rs"), "pub fn gen() {}").unwrap();
254: }
255: 
256: // cc crate para compilar C
257: fn main() {
258:     cc::Build::new()
259:         .file("src/c_code.c")
260:         .compile("my_c_code");
261: }
262: ```
263: 
264: ## Publishing
265: 
266: ```bash
267: cargo login                              # autentica
268: cargo publish --dry-run                  # simula
269: cargo package --list                     # lista arquivos do .crate
270: cargo publish                            # publica
271: cargo yank --version 1.0.3               # remove da lista
272: cargo yank --version 1.0.3 --undo        # desfaz yank
273: cargo owner --add github:myorg:myrepo    # add owner
274: ```
275: 
276: ## Config (.cargo/config.toml)
277: 
278: ```toml
279: # ~/.cargo/config.toml ou .cargo/config.toml no projeto
280: 
281: [alias]
282: c = "check"
283: b = "build"
284: r = "run"
285: t = "test"
286: cl = "clippy"
287: 
288: [build]
289: jobs = 4
290: rustc-wrapper = "sccache"
291: target-dir = "/tmp/cargo-targets"
292: incremental = true
293: 
294: [target.x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu]
295: linker = "clang"
296: rustflags = ["-C", "target-cpu=native"]
297: 
298: [env]
299: RUST_LOG = "debug"
300: 
301: [http]
302: proxy = "http://proxy:8080"
303: timeout = 30
304: 
305: [net]
306: git-fetch-with-cli = true
307: offline = true
308: 
309: [term]
310: quiet = false
311: verbose = true
312: color = "auto"
313: 
314: [registries.my-registry]
315: index = "https://my-registry.com/index"
316: token = "..."
317: 
318: [registry]
319: default = "my-registry"
320: 
321: [source.crates-io]
322: replace-with = "mirror"
323: 
324: [source.mirror]
325: registry = "https://mirror.example.com/cargo"
326: ```
327: 
328: ### Config via CLI
329: 
330: ```bash
331: cargo build --config 'build.jobs=8'
332: cargo run --config 'registries.my-registry.index="https://..."'
333: cargo test --config /path/to/config.toml
334: ```
335: 
336: ## Environment Variables
337: 
338: ```bash
339: # Lidas pelo Cargo
340: CARGO_HOME=~/.cargo
341: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/target
342: RUSTFLAGS="-C target-cpu=native"
343: RUSTC_WRAPPER=sccache
344: CARGO_INCREMENTAL=1
345: 
346: # Setadas para o build
347: CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR=/project
348: CARGO_PKG_VERSION=0.1.0
349: CARGO_PKG_NAME=my_app
350: CARGO_CRATE_NAME=my_app
351: CARGO_BIN_NAME=cli
352: OUT_DIR=/project/target/debug/build/my_app-abc/out
353: TARGET=x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
354: HOST=x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
355: NUM_JOBS=8
356: OPT_LEVEL=0
357: DEBUG=true
358: PROFILE=debug
359: ```
360: 
361: ## Lints
362: 
363: ```toml
364: [lints.rust]
365: unsafe_code = "forbid"
366: missing_docs = "warn"
367: unused_imports = "deny"
368: clippy::pedantic = "warn"
369: clippy::unwrap_used = "deny"
370: clippy::expect_used = "deny"
371: clippy::panic = "deny"
372: 
373: # Workspace-level
374: [workspace.lints]
375: rust.unsafe_code = "forbid"
376: ```
377: 
378: ## Boas Práticas (Cargo)
379: 
380: 1. **Commit `Cargo.lock`** para aplicações (não para bibliotecas)
381: 2. **`rust-version`** no Cargo.toml para MSRV claro
382: 3. **`publish = false`** para apps internos
383: 4. **Workspaces** para monorepos — dependências compartilhadas
384: 5. **`resolver = "2"`** sempre (Edition 2021+)
385: 6. **Features `default` mínimas** — não force dependências extras
386: 7. **`dep:` prefix** para features que expõem dependências opcionais
387: 8. **`[lints]` no workspace** para consistência
388: 9. **`cargo deny`** para auditoria de licenças e segurança
389: 10. **`[profile.release]` otimizado** — LTO, strip, codegen-units=1

(End of file - total 389 lines)
</content>

**Title:** ai_guides/rust_guide/16-project-practical.md

<path>/projects/my/redmine-wrapper-rs/ai_guides/rust_guide/16-project-practical.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # 16 — Projeto Prático: Guia de Arquitetura
2: 
3: ## Decisões Arquiteturais
4: 
5: ### Quando usar o quê
6: 
7: | Problema | Solução |
8: |----------|---------|
9: | CLI simples | `clap` + `std::io` |
10: | Web server | `axum` + `tokio` + `sqlx` |
11: | API client | `reqwest` + `serde` |
12: | GUI desktop | `egui` / `dioxus` / `slint` |
13: | GUI web | `leptos` / `yew` / `dioxus` |
14: | Embedded | `no_std` + `embassy` |
15: | WebAssembly | `wasm-bindgen` + `wasm-pack` |
16: | Game | `bevy` |
17: | Async runtime | `tokio` (default), `async-std`, `smol` |
18: | Serialization | `serde` (standard) |
19: | Error handling | `thiserror` (lib) / `anyhow` (app) |
20: | Logging | `tracing` / `log` + `env_logger` |
21: | Testing | `criterion` (bench) / `proptest` (property) |
22: | Database | `sqlx` (async, compile-time checked) / `diesel` |
23: | ORM | `sea-orm` / `diesel` |
24: | HTTP client | `reqwest` (high-level) / `hyper` (low-level) |
25: | HTTP server | `axum` / `actix-web` / `warp` |
26: 
27: ### Padrão: Biblioteca + Binário
28: 
29: ```rust
30: // src/lib.rs — toda a lógica
31: pub mod config;
32: pub mod models;
33: pub mod services;
34: pub mod db;
35: 
36: // src/main.rs — thin wrapper
37: use my_app::{config, services};
38: 
39: #[tokio::main]
40: async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
41:     let cfg = config::load()?;
42:     services::run(cfg).await?;
43:     Ok(())
44: }
45: ```
46: 
47: ### Separando em Crates
48: 
49: ```
50: my_app/
51: ├── Cargo.toml                # workspace root (sem [package])
52: ├── crates/
53: │   ├── core/                 # tipos, traits, interfaces
54: │   ├── domain/               # lógica de negócio
55: │   ├── infra/                # DB, HTTP client, file system
56: │   ├── api/                  # endpoints HTTP (axum)
57: │   └── cli/                  # CLI (clap)
58: ```
59: 
60: ## Criação de Projeto Passo a Passo
61: 
62: ```bash
63: cargo new my_app
64: cd my_app
65: cargo add tokio --features full
66: cargo add serde --features derive
67: cargo add anyhow thiserror
68: cargo add clap --features derive
69: cargo add tracing tracing-subscriber
70: ```
71: 
72: ### Cargo.toml final
73: 
74: ```toml
75: [package]
76: name = "my_app"
77: version = "0.1.0"
78: edition = "2024"
79: rust-version = "1.85"
80: 
81: [dependencies]
82: tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
83: serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
84: anyhow = "1"
85: thiserror = "2"
86: clap = { version = "4", features = ["derive"] }
87: tracing = "0.1"
88: tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"] }
89: reqwest = { version = "0.12", features = ["json"] }
90: sqlx = { version = "0.8", features = ["runtime-tokio", "postgres"] }
91: axum = "0.7"
92: 
93: [dev-dependencies]
94: criterion = "0.5"
95: tokio-test = "0.4"
96: 
97: [profile.release]
98: opt-level = 3
99: lto = "fat"
100: codegen-units = 1
101: strip = "symbols"
102: ```
103: 
104: ### Entry Point (main.rs)
105: 
106: ```rust
107: use clap::Parser;
108: use tracing_subscriber::EnvFilter;
109: 
110: #[derive(Parser)]
111: #[command(name = "my_app", about = "My Rust application")]
112: struct Cli {
113:     #[arg(short, long)]
114:     config: Option<String>,
115: 
116:     #[command(subcommand)]
117:     command: Option<Command>,
118: }
119: 
120: #[derive(clap::Subcommand)]
121: enum Command {
122:     Serve { #[arg(default_value = "3000")] port: u16 },
123:     Migrate,
124: }
125: 
126: #[tokio::main]
127: async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {
128:     tracing_subscriber::fmt()
129:         .with_env_filter(EnvFilter::from_default_env())
130:         .init();
131: 
132:     let cli = Cli::parse();
133: 
134:     match cli.command {
135:         Some(Command::Serve { port }) => run_server(port).await?,
136:         Some(Command::Migrate) => run_migrations().await?,
137:         None => run_server(3000).await?,
138:     }
139: 
140:     Ok(())
141: }
142: ```
143: 
144: ## Padrões de Projeto em Rust
145: 
146: ### Builder Pattern
147: 
148: ```rust
149: #[derive(Default)]
150: struct ConfigBuilder {
151:     host: Option<String>,
152:     port: Option<u16>,
153:     timeout: Option<Duration>,
154: }
155: 
156: impl ConfigBuilder {
157:     fn host(mut self, host: &str) -> Self {
158:         self.host = Some(host.to_string());
159:         self
160:     }
161:     fn port(mut self, port: u16) -> Self {
162:         self.port = Some(port);
163:         self
164:     }
165:     fn build(self) -> Config {
166:         Config {
167:             host: self.host.unwrap_or("localhost".into()),
168:             port: self.port.unwrap_or(8080),
169:             timeout: self.timeout.unwrap_or(Duration::from_secs(30)),
170:         }
171:     }
172: }
173: ```
174: 
175: ### Newtype Pattern
176: 
177: ```rust
178: #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
179: pub struct Email(String);
180: 
181: impl Email {
182:     pub fn new(s: &str) -> Result<Self, String> {
183:         if s.contains('@') {
184:             Ok(Email(s.to_string()))
185:         } else {
186:             Err("invalid email".into())
187:         }
188:     }
189:     pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str { &self.0 }
190: }
191: 
192: // Serde support via custom impl
193: impl<'de> Deserialize<'de> for Email {
194:     fn deserialize<D>(deserializer: D) -> Result<Self, D::Error>
195:     where D: Deserializer<'de> {
196:         let s = String::deserialize(deserializer)?;
197:         Email::new(&s).map_err(serde::de::Error::custom)
198:     }
199: }
200: ```
201: 
202: ### Repository Pattern
203: 
204: ```rust
205: #[async_trait]
206: pub trait UserRepository: Send + Sync {
207:     async fn find_by_id(&self, id: Uuid) -> Result<Option<User>, AppError>;
208:     async fn save(&self, user: &User) -> Result<(), AppError>;
209:     async fn delete(&self, id: Uuid) -> Result<(), AppError>;
210: }
211: 
212: // Postgres implementation
213: pub struct PgUserRepository {
214:     pool: PgPool,
215: }
216: 
217: #[async_trait]
218: impl UserRepository for PgUserRepository {
219:     async fn find_by_id(&self, id: Uuid) -> Result<Option<User>, AppError> {
220:         let user = sqlx::query_as!(
221:             User,
222:             "SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE id = $1",
223:             id,
224:         )
225:         .fetch_optional(&self.pool)
226:         .await?;
227:         Ok(user)
228:     }
229:     // ...
230: }
231: ```
232: 
233: ### Error Handling Pattern
234: 
235: ```rust
236: #[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]
237: pub enum AppError {
238:     #[error("not found: {0}")]
239:     NotFound(String),
240: 
241:     #[error("validation error: {0}")]
242:     Validation(String),
243: 
244:     #[error("database error: {0}")]
245:     Database(#[from] sqlx::Error),
246: 
247:     #[error("internal error: {0}")]
248:     Internal(#[from] anyhow::Error),
249: }
250: 
251: // Axum integration
252: impl IntoResponse for AppError {
253:     fn into_response(self) -> axum::response::Response {
254:         let (status, message) = match &self {
255:             AppError::NotFound(_) => (StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, self.to_string()),
256:             AppError::Validation(_) => (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, self.to_string()),
257:             AppError::Database(_) => (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, self.to_string()),
258:             AppError::Internal(_) => (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, self.to_string()),
259:         };
260:         (status, Json(json!({ "error": message }))).into_response()
261:     }
262: }
263: ```
264: 
265: ### State Pattern (Tipos de Estado)
266: 
267: ```rust
268: // Em vez de runtime checks, codifique no type system
269: mod draft { pub struct Draft; }
270: mod published { pub struct Published; }
271: 
272: struct Post<State> {
273:     title: String,
274:     content: String,
275:     _state: std::marker::PhantomData<State>,
276: }
277: 
278: impl Post<draft::Draft> {
279:     pub fn new(title: &str) -> Self {
280:         Self { title: title.into(), content: String::new(), _state: PhantomData }
281:     }
282:     pub fn add_content(&mut self, text: &str) { self.content.push_str(text); }
283:     pub fn publish(self) -> Post<published::Published> {
284:         Post { title: self.title, content: self.content, _state: PhantomData }
285:     }
286: }
287: 
288: impl Post<published::Published> {
289:     pub fn content(&self) -> &str { &self.content }
290: }
291: ```
292: 
293: ## Logging e Observabilidade
294: 
295: ```rust
296: use tracing::{info, error, warn, debug, span, Level, instrument};
297: 
298: #[instrument(skip(pool))]
299: async fn process_user(pool: &PgPool, user_id: Uuid) -> Result<(), AppError> {
300:     info!("processing user {user_id}");
301: 
302:     let user = find_user(pool, user_id).await?;
303:     debug!(?user, "found user");
304: 
305:     warn!("deprecated code path for user {user_id}");
306: 
307:     Ok(())
308: }
309: 
310: // Structured fields
311: info!(
312:     user_id = %user.id,
313:     email = %user.email,
314:     duration_ms = %elapsed.as_millis(),
315:     "request completed"
316: );
317: ```
318: 
319: ## Performance
320: 
321: ```rust
322: // Pre-allocate
323: let mut v = Vec::with_capacity(1000);
324: let mut s = String::with_capacity(100);
325: 
326: // Avoid clones
327: fn process(data: &[u8]) { /* ... */ }  // borrow instead of &Vec<u8>
328: 
329: // Small string optimization (smol_str, smartstring)
330: // interned strings (intern)
331: 
332: // Profile
333: // cargo build --release && perf record ./target/release/my_app
334: // cargo flamegraph
335: 
336: // Memory
337: // jemalloc / mimalloc allocator
338: use tikv_jemallocator::Jemalloc;
339: #[global_allocator]
340: static GLOBAL: Jemalloc = Jemalloc;
341: 
342: // Cache-friendly data layout
343: // AoS → SoA para acesso sequencial
344: struct AoS { x: f32, y: f32, z: f32 } // Array of Structs
345: struct SoA { xs: Vec<f32>, ys: Vec<f32>, zs: Vec<f32> } // Struct of Arrays
346: ```
347: 
348: ## CI/CD Completo
349: 
350: ```yaml
351: name: CI
352: on: [push, pull_request]
353: 
354: env:
355:   CARGO_TERM_COLOR: always
356:   RUSTFLAGS: "-D warnings"
357: 
358: jobs:
359:   check:
360:     name: Check
361:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
362:     steps:
363:       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
364:       - uses: actions-rust-lang/setup-rust-toolchain@v1
365:         with:
366:           toolchain: stable
367:           components: clippy, rustfmt
368:       - run: cargo fmt -- --check
369:       - run: cargo clippy -- -D warnings
370:       - run: cargo check
371: 
372:   test:
373:     name: Test
374:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
375:     steps:
376:       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
377:       - uses: actions-rust-lang/setup-rust-toolchain@v1
378:       - run: cargo test
379:       - run: cargo test --doc
380: 
381:   coverage:
382:     name: Code Coverage
383:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
384:     steps:
385:       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
386:       - uses: actions-rust-lang/setup-rust-toolchain@v1
387:       - run: cargo install cargo-tarpaulin
388:       - run: cargo tarpaulin --out Xml
389:       - uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4
390: 
391:   security:
392:     name: Security Audit
393:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
394:     steps:
395:       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
396:       - uses: actions-rust-lang/setup-rust-toolchain@v1
397:       - run: cargo install cargo-audit
398:       - run: cargo audit
399: 
400:   build:
401:     name: Build Release
402:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
403:     steps:
404:       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
405:       - uses: actions-rust-lang/setup-rust-toolchain@v1
406:       - run: cargo build --release
407:       - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
408:         with:
409:           name: binary
410:           path: target/release/my_app
411: ```
412: 
413: ## Boas Práticas (Projeto)
414: 
415: 1. **Organizar por domínio**, não por camada técnica
416: 2. **Lib + thin binary** — facilita testes e reuso
417: 3. **Separar commands/dao/services** em módulos claros
418: 4. **Usar `anyhow` em app, `thiserror` em lib**
419: 5. **Injeção de dependência via traits** para testabilidade
420: 6. **Testes de integração com `sqlx::test` ou testcontainers**
421: 7. **Documentar decisões** em ADRs (Architecture Decision Records)
422: 8. **`cargo deny`** para auditoria automática
423: 9. **Benchmarks via criterion** para código crítico
424: 10. **Perfil de release otimizado** antes de deploy

(End of file - total 424 lines)
</content>
```

244: 
245: /// Wrapper para requisições de criação de issue.
246: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
247: pub(crate) struct CreateIssueWrapper {
248:     /// Payload de criação encapsulado.
249:     pub issue: CreateIssuePayload,
250: }
251: 
252: /// Wrapper para requisições de atualização de issue.
253: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]
254: pub(crate) struct UpdateIssueWrapper {
255:     /// Payload de atualização encapsulado.
256:     pub issue: UpdateIssuePayload,
257: }

(End of file - total 257 lines)

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
2: // License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
3: // file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.
4: 
5: /// Converte um filtro serializável em pares chave-valor para query string.
6: ///
7: /// Útil para enviar filtros estruturados como parâmetros de URL.
8: pub fn filter_to_query<T: serde::Serialize>(
9:     filter: Option<&T>,
10: ) -> Vec<(String, String)> {
11:     match filter {
12:         None => Vec::new(),
13:         Some(f) => {
14:             let value = serde_json::to_value(f).unwrap_or_default();
15:             flatten_value("", &value)
16:         }
17:     }
18: }
19: 
20: /// Achata recursivamente um `serde_json::Value` em pares chave=valor.
21: fn flatten_value(prefix: &str, value: &serde_json::Value) -> Vec<(String, String)> {
22:     match value {
23:         serde_json::Value::Object(map) => {
24:             let mut result = Vec::new();
25:             for (key, val) in map {
26:                 let new_prefix = if prefix.is_empty() {
27:                     key.clone()
28:                 } else {
29:                     format!("{}[{}]", prefix, key)
30:                 };
31:                 result.extend(flatten_value(&new_prefix, val));
32:             }
33:             result
34:         }
35:         serde_json::Value::Array(arr) => {
36:             let mut result = Vec::new();
37:             for (i, val) in arr.iter().enumerate() {
38:                 let new_prefix = format!("{}[{}]", prefix, i);
39:                 result.extend(flatten_value(&new_prefix, val));
40:             }
41:             result
42:         }
43:         serde_json::Value::String(s) => {
44:             vec![(prefix.to_string(), s.clone())]
45:         }
46:         serde_json::Value::Number(n) => {
47:             vec![(prefix.to_string(), n.to_string())]
48:         }
49:         serde_json::Value::Bool(b) => {
50:             vec![(prefix.to_string(), if *b { "1" } else { "0" }.to_string())]
51:         }
52:         serde_json::Value::Null => Vec::new(),
53:     }
54: }
55: 
56: #[cfg(test)]
57: mod tests {
58:     use super::*;
59:     use serde::Serialize;
60: 
61:     #[derive(Debug, Serialize)]
62:     struct TestFilter {
63:         name: Option<String>,
64:         active: Option<bool>,
65:     }
66: 
67:     #[test]
68:     fn test_filter_to_query_none() {
69:         let result = filter_to_query::<TestFilter>(None);
70:         assert!(result.is_empty());
71:     }
72: 
73:     #[test]
74:     fn test_flatten_simple() {
75:         let f = TestFilter {
76:             name: Some("test".into()),
77:             active: Some(true),
78:         };
79:         let result = filter_to_query(Some(&f));
80:         assert!(result.contains(&("name".to_string(), "test".to_string())));
81:         assert!(result.contains(&("active".to_string(), "1".to_string())));
82:     }
83: }

(End of file - total 83 lines)
</content>
```

<path>/projects/my/redmine-wrapper-rs/ai_guides/rust_guide/08-closures-iterators.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # 8 — Closures e Iterators (Programação Funcional)
2: 
3: ## Closures
4: 
5: Closures são funções anônimas que capturam o ambiente:
6: 
7: ```rust
8: // Sintaxe |args| body
9: let add_one = |x: i32| -> i32 { x + 1 };
10: let add_one = |x| x + 1;                // type inference
11: 
12: // Exige anotação de tipo se não houver contexto
13: let identity = |x: &str| -> &str { x };
14: ```
15: 
16: ### Captura do Ambiente
17: 
18: ```rust
19: let x = 5;
20: 
21: // Captura por referência imutável (Fn)
22: let print_x = || println!("{x}");
23: 
24: // Captura por referência mutável (FnMut)
25: let mut move_x = || { x += 1; };
26: 
27: // Captura por valor (FnOnce) — move x para dentro da closure
28: let consume_x = || { drop(x); };
29: ```
30: 
31: ### Closure Traits (Fn, FnMut, FnOnce)
32: 
33: | Trait | Captura | Chama | Usado para |
34: |-------|---------|-------|------------|
35: | `FnOnce` | por valor | 1x | Consumir capturas |
36: | `FnMut` | &mut ref | várias | Mutar capturas |
37: | `Fn` | & ref | várias | Só ler |
38: 
39: ```rust
40: fn call_fn<F: Fn()>(f: F) { f(); f(); }
41: fn call_fn_mut<F: FnMut()>(mut f: F) { f(); f(); }
42: fn call_fn_once<F: FnOnce()>(f: F) { f(); }
43: 
44: // Toda closure implementa FnOnce
45: // Toda closure que não move, implementa FnMut
46: // Toda closure que não move + não muta, implementa Fn
47: ```
48: 
49: ### move Keyword
50: 
51: Força a closure a tomar ownership das capturas:
52: 
53: ```rust
54: let x = vec![1, 2, 3];
55: 
56: // Sem move — borrow, precisa viver mais que closure
57: let equal = |z| z == x;
58: 
59: // Com move — ownership transferido
60: thread::spawn(move || {
61:     println!("{x:?}");
62: }).join().unwrap();
63: ```
64: 
65: ### Retornando Closures
66: 
67: ```rust
68: fn returns_closure() -> impl Fn(i32) -> i32 {
69:     |x| x + 1
70:     // move é necessário se captura ambiente
71: }
72: 
73: // Retornando closures com captura
74: fn make_adder(x: i32) -> impl Fn(i32) -> i32 {
75:     move |y| x + y
76: }
77: ```
78: 
79: ### Closures em Structs
80: 
81: ```rust
82: struct Cacher<T, R>
83: where
84:     T: Fn(u32) -> R,
85: {
86:     calculation: T,
87:     value: Option<R>,
88: }
89: ```
90: 
91: ## Iterators
92: 
93: O trait `Iterator`:
94: 
95: ```rust
96: pub trait Iterator {
97:     type Item;
98:     fn next(&mut self) -> Option<Self::Item>;
99:     // muitas default methods...
100: }
101: ```
102: 
103: ### Creating Iterators
104: 
105: ```rust
106: let v = vec![1, 2, 3];
107: 
108: v.iter()         // &T (borrow)
109: v.iter_mut()     // &mut T (mutable borrow)
110: v.into_iter()    // T (consumes v)
111: 
112: // De ranges
113: (0..10).step_by(2)
114: 
115: // De Option
116: let iter = Some(42).into_iter();
117: 
118: // Custom iterator
119: struct Counter { count: u32 }
120: impl Iterator for Counter {
121:     type Item = u32;
122:     fn next(&mut self) -> Option<Self::Item> {
123:         self.count += 1;
124:         if self.count < 6 { Some(self.count) } else { None }
125:     }
126: }
127: ```
128: 
129: ### Consuming Adaptors (consomem o iterator)
130: 
131: ```rust
132: let sum: u32 = v.iter().sum();
133: let count = v.iter().count();
134: let max = v.iter().max();
135: let min = v.iter().min();
136: let last = v.iter().last();
137: let nth = v.iter().nth(2);
138: let collected: Vec<_> = v.iter().collect();
139: let reduced = v.iter().fold(0, |acc, x| acc + x);
140: let all_gt = v.iter().all(|x| x > &0);
141: let any_gt = v.iter().any(|x| x > &5);
142: let position = v.iter().position(|x| x == &3);
143: ```
144: 
145: ### Iterator Adaptors (retornam novos iterators) — lazy!
146: 
147: ```rust
148: v.iter()
149:     .map(|x| x * 2)             // transforma cada elemento
150:     .filter(|x| x > &5)         // mantém só os que satisfazem
151:     .take(3)                     // pega N elementos
152:     .skip(2)                     // pula N elementos
153:     .step_by(2)                  // pula de 2 em 2
154:     .flatten()                   // achata nested iterators
155:     .flat_map(|x| 0..*x)        // map + flatten
156:     .enumerate()                 // (index, element)
157:     .zip(other_iter)             // combina com outro iterator
158:     .chain(other_iter)           // concatena iterators
159:     .rev()                       // reverse
160:     .cycle()                     // loop infinito
161:     .inspect(|x| println!("{x}")) // debug
162:     .peekable()                  // peek sem consumir
163:     .collect::<Vec<_>>();        // materializa
164: ```
165: 
166: ### Partition
167: 
168: ```rust
169: let (even, odd): (Vec<_>, Vec<_>) = (0..10).partition(|x| x % 2 == 0);
170: ```
171: 
172: ### Unzip
173: 
174: ```rust
175: let pairs = vec![(1, "a"), (2, "b")];
176: let (nums, strs): (Vec<_>, Vec<_>) = pairs.into_iter().unzip();
177: ```
178: 
179: ### FilterMap
180: 
181: ```rust
182: // filter + map em uma passada só
183: let vals = ["1", "two", "3", "four"];
184: let nums: Vec<i32> = vals.iter()
185:     .filter_map(|s| s.parse().ok())
186:     .collect();
187: ```
188: 
189: ### Combinando Closures e Iterators
190: 
191: ```rust
192: struct Shoe { size: u32, style: String }
193: 
194: fn shoes_in_size(shoes: Vec<Shoe>, shoe_size: u32) -> Vec<Shoe> {
195:     shoes.into_iter()
196:         .filter(|s| s.size == shoe_size)
197:         .collect()
198: }
199: 
200: // Uso: `for` loop vs pipeline (são equivalentes)
201: ```
202: 
203: ### IntoIterator
204: 
205: ```rust
206: // for x in v equivale a v.into_iter()
207: // Arrays (Edition 2021+)
208: let arr = [1, 2, 3];
209: for x in arr {}  // OK — Edition 2021
210: 
211: // Implementando IntoIterator pra tipo customizado
212: struct Grid(Vec<Vec<i32>>);
213: impl IntoIterator for Grid {
214:     type Item = i32;
215:     type IntoIter = std::vec::IntoIter<i32>;
216:     fn into_iter(self) -> Self::IntoIter {
217:         self.0.into_iter().flatten().collect::<Vec<_>>().into_iter()
218:     }
219: }
220: ```
221: 
222: ## Zero-Cost Abstractions
223: 
224: Iterators e closures **não adicionam overhead** em relação a loops
225: manuais. O compilador otimiza o pipeline em um único loop.
226: 
227: ```rust
228: // Este código:
229: let sum: i32 = (0..1000)
230:     .filter(|x| x % 2 == 0)
231:     .map(|x| x * 2)
232:     .sum();
233: 
234: // Compila para o mesmo assembly que:
235: let mut sum = 0;
236: for x in 0..1000 {
237:     if x % 2 == 0 {
238:         sum += x * 2;
239:     }
240: }
241: ```
242: 
243: ## Boas Práticas
244: 
245: 1. **Preferir pipelines de iterators** a loops manuais — mais
246:    expressivo e compila igual
247: 2. **Usar `filter_map`** para filter + map em uma passada
248: 3. **`collect::<Vec<_>>()`** só quando precisar materializar
249: 4. **Lazy evaluation** — iterators não executam até serem consumidos
250: 5. **`enumerate()`** substitui `for i in 0..v.len() {}`
251: 6. **Closures pequenas podem ser `Fn`** — prefira à mais genérica
252: 7. **`move`** é necessário para threads, async, ou retornar closure
253: 8. **Evitar `for` em favor de `map`/`filter`/`fold`** em cadeias curtas
254: 9. **Usar `any()`/`all()`** em vez de loops com flag bool
255: 10. **`flat_map()`** para nested loops

(End of file - total 255 lines)
</content>
```

**Title:** ai_guides/rust_guide/09-modules-crates.md

  "filePath": "/projects/my/redmine-wrapper-rs/ai_guides/rust_guide/09-modules-crates.md"
}
```

**Output:**
```
<path>/projects/my/redmine-wrapper-rs/ai_guides/rust_guide/09-modules-crates.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # 9 — Módulos, Pacotes e Crates
2: 
3: ## Pacotes e Crates
4: 
5: - **Crate**: menor quantidade de código que o compilador considera.
6:   Pode ser binário (`main.rs`) ou biblioteca (`lib.rs`).
7: - **Pacote**: um ou mais crates com um `Cargo.toml`. Pode ter 0 ou 1
8:   library crate + vários binary crates.
9: 
10: ```
11: my_package/
12: ├── Cargo.toml               # define o pacote
13: ├── src/
14: │   ├── lib.rs               # library crate root
15: │   ├── main.rs              # binary crate root (mesmo nome da lib)
16: │   └── bin/                 # binary crates adicionais
17: │       ├── server.rs        # binário "server"
18: │       └── cli.rs           # binário "cli"
19: ```
20: 
21: ```toml
22: [package]
23: name = "my_app"
24: version = "0.1.0"
25: edition = "2024"
26: 
27: # automaticamente: lib + bin com mesmo nome
28: # bin extras:
29: [[bin]]
30: name = "server"
31: path = "src/bin/server.rs"
32: ```
33: 
34: ## Módulos: Organização Interna
35: 
36: ### Definição
37: 
38: Módulos organizam o código dentro de um crate:
39: 
40: ```rust
41: // src/lib.rs
42: mod front_of_house {
43:     pub mod hosting {
44:         pub fn add_to_waitlist() {}
45:         fn seat_at_table() {}       // privado
46:     }
47: 
48:     mod serving {
49:         fn take_order() {}
50:     }
51: }
52: ```
53: 
54: ### Árvore de Módulos
55: 
56: ```
57: crate
58:  └── front_of_house
59:      ├── hosting
60:      │   └── add_to_waitlist
61:      └── serving
62:          └── take_order
63: ```
64: 
65: ### Visibility (`pub`)
66: 
67: | Modificador | Visível em |
68: |-------------|------------|
69:  | (nada) | privado (módulo pai e irmãos) |
70:  | `pub` | qualquer um |
71:  | `pub(crate)` | dentro do crate |
72:  | `pub(super)` | módulo pai |
73:  | `pub(in path)` | path específico |
74: 
75: ```rust
76: mod outer {
77:     fn private() {}
78:     pub fn public() {}
79:     pub(crate) fn crate_visible() {}
80: 
81:     mod inner {
82:         pub(super) fn parent_visible() {} // visível em outer
83:         pub(in crate::outer) fn specific() {}
84:     }
85: }
86: ```
87: 
88: ### Struct Visibility
89: 
90: ```rust
91: mod building {
92:     pub struct Window {
93:         pub width: u32,      // público
94:         height: u32,         // privado — só criado internamente
95:     }
96: 
97:     impl Window {
98:         pub fn new(w: u32, h: u32) -> Window {  // construtor público
99:             Window { width: w, height: h }
100:         }
101:     }
102: }
103: ```
104: 
105: ## Paths: Referenciando Itens
106: 
107: ```rust
108: // Absoluto (começa com crate)
109: crate::front_of_house::hosting::add_to_waitlist();
110: 
111: // Relativo (self, super)
112: self::front_of_house::hosting::add_to_waitlist();
113: super::serve_order();
114: ```
115: 
116: ### use Keyword
117: 
118: ```rust
119: // Traz path para o escopo
120: use crate::front_of_house::hosting;
121: hosting::add_to_waitlist();
122: 
123: // Item diretamente
124: use crate::front_of_house::hosting::add_to_waitlist;
125: add_to_waitlist();
126: 
127: // Alias
128: use std::fmt::Result as FmtResult;
129: 
130: // Re-export
131: pub use crate::front_of_house::hosting; // público!
132: 
133: // Nested paths
134: use std::{cmp::Ordering, io::{self, Write}};
135: 
136: // Glob
137: use std::collections::*;  // evite em produção
138: ```
139: 
140: ## Separando em Arquivos
141: 
142: ```rust
143: // src/lib.rs
144: mod front_of_house;       // procura front_of_house.rs ou
145:                           // front_of_house/mod.rs
146: 
147: // src/front_of_house.rs ou src/front_of_house/mod.rs
148: pub mod hosting;          // procura hosting.rs ou
149:                           // front_of_house/hosting.rs
150: 
151: // src/front_of_house/hosting.rs
152: pub fn add_to_waitlist() {}
153: ```
154: 
155: ### Estrutura recomendada (Rust Edition 2021+)
156: 
157: ```
158: src/
159: ├── lib.rs               # crate root
160: ├── main.rs              # binário (opcional)
161: ├── front_of_house/
162: │   ├── mod.rs           # mod front_of_house
163: │   └── hosting.rs       # pub mod hosting
164: └── serving.rs           # mod serving
165: ```
166: 
167: ## Workspaces (Múltiplos Pacotes)
168: 
169: ```toml
170: # Cargo.toml (workspace root — sem [package])
171: [workspace]
172: members = [
173:     "crates/backend",
174:     "crates/frontend",
175:     "crates/common",
176: ]
177: resolver = "2"
178: 
179: [workspace.package]
180: version = "0.1.0"
181: edition = "2024"
182: 
183: [workspace.dependencies]
184: serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
185: tokio = "1"
186: ```
187: 
188: ```toml
189: # crates/common/Cargo.toml
190: [package]
191: name = "common"
192: version.workspace = true
193: edition.workspace = true
194: 
195: [dependencies]
196: serde.workspace = true
197: ```
198: 
199: ```bash
200: cargo build -p backend     # build específico
201: cargo test -p common       # test específico
202: ```
203: 
204: ## Features
205: 
206: ```toml
207: [features]
208: default = ["std"]
209: std = []
210: serde = ["dep:serde"]      # dep: prefix evita feature implícita
211: full = ["std", "serde", "async"]
212: 
213: # Optional dependencies criam features implícitas
214: [dependencies]
215: serde = { version = "1", optional = true }
216: ```
217: 
218: ```rust
219: // Em runtime
220: #[cfg(feature = "serde")]
221: impl Serialize for MyType { ... }
222: 
223: #[cfg(not(feature = "std"))]
224: extern crate alloc;
225: 
226: // Max 300 features (crates.io)
227: ```
228: 
229: ## Conditional Compilation
230: 
231: ```rust
232: #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
233: fn platform_specific() { /* linux */ }
234: 
235: #[cfg(not(target_os = "windows"))]
236: fn fallback() {}
237: 
238: #[cfg(any(unix, target_os = "wasi"))]
239: fn unix_like() {}
240: 
241: #[cfg(feature = "nightly")]
242: fn uses_nightly() {}
243: 
244: // cfg! macro — runtime check
245: if cfg!(target_arch = "wasm32") {
246:     // web-specific branch
247: }
248: ```
249: 
250: ## Boas Práticas
251: 
252: 1. **Estrutura de módulos reflete a API pública** — não a organização
253:    interna
254: 2. **`pub use`** para exportar API limpa — esconda detalhes internos
255: 3. **Privado por default** — só exponha o necessário
256: 4. **Préfira paths absolutos (`crate::`)** — mais fáceis de mover
257: 5. **Workspaces** para projetos grandes (monorepo)
258: 6. **Features** para funcionalidades opcionais (sem breaking)
259: 7. **`dep:` prefix** (Edition 2021+) para dependências opcionais
260: 8. **Evite glob imports** — polui o namespace
261: 9. **Um módulo por arquivo** — arquivos grandes (>400 linhos) devem
262:    ser divididos
263: 10. **`build.rs`** para geração de código, linking com C, ou
264:     detecção de plataforma

(End of file - total 264 lines)
</content>
```

<content>
1: # 1 — Toolchain, Ferramentas e Setup
2: 
3: ## rustup (gerenciador de toolchain)
4: 
5: ```bash
6: # Instalar
7: curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
8: 
9: # Gerenciar toolchains
10: rustup show                     # toolchains e targets instalados
11: rustup update                   # atualizar tudo
12: rustup default nightly          # mudar default para nightly
13: rustup toolchain install nightly
14: rustup target add wasm32-unknown-unknown
15: 
16: # Múltiplas versões
17: rustup run nightly cargo build  # executar com nightly
18: cargo +nightly build            # shorthand
19: ```
20: 
21: ## rustc (compilador)
22: 
23: ```rust
24: // hello.rs
25: fn main() {
26:     println!("Hello, Rust 1.97.0!");
27: }
28: ```
29: 
30: ```bash
31: rustc hello.rs                  # compilação direta
32: rustc -O hello.rs               # com otimizações
33: rustc --edition 2024 hello.rs   # edição específica
34: ```
35: 
36: ## Cargo (package manager + build system)
37: 
38: ```bash
39: cargo new my_project              # novo projeto binário
40: cargo new my_lib --lib            # novo projeto lib
41: cargo init                        # em diretório existente
42: cargo build                       # debug build
43: cargo build --release             # release build (otimizado)
44: cargo check                       # só verifica tipos (mais rápido que build)
45: cargo run                         # build + executar
46: cargo test                        # rodar testes
47: cargo doc --open                  # documentação
48: cargo clippy                      # lints extras
49: cargo fmt                         # formatação automática
50: ```
51: 
52: ### Cargo.toml mínimo
53: 
54: ```toml
55: [package]
56: name = "my_app"
57: version = "0.1.0"
58: edition = "2024"
59: rust-version = "1.85"
60: description = "A brief description"
61: license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
62: 
63: [dependencies]
64: serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
65: tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
66: ```
67: 
68: ### Optimized release profile
69: 
70: ```toml
71: [profile.release]
72: opt-level = 3          # -O3
73: lto = "fat"            # link-time optimization
74: codegen-units = 1      # melhor otimização (compilação mais lenta)
75: strip = "symbols"      # remover símbolos de debug
76: debug = false          # sem info de debug em release
77: panic = "abort"        # binário menor, sem unwinding
78: ```
79: 
80: ## rust-analyzer (IDE)
81: 
82: - LSP server oficial para Rust
83: - Suportado por VS Code, Neovim (via `neovim/nvim-lspconfig`),
84:   JetBrains (intrinsic), Zed, Helix
85: - Funcionalidades: autocomplete, go-to-definition, find references,
86:   inline type hints, code actions, rename, diagnostics
87: 
88: ## Clippy (lints)
89: 
90: ```bash
91: cargo clippy                     # todos os lints
92: cargo clippy -- -W clippy::pedantic   # mais rigoroso
93: cargo clippy -- -W clippy::nursery    # ainda instáveis
94: cargo clippy --fix               # auto-fix
95: ```
96: 
97: ```toml
98: # Cargo.toml (opcional - lint config)
99: [lints.rust]
100: unsafe_code = "forbid"
101: missing_docs = "warn"
102: 
103: [lints.clippy]
104: pedantic = "warn"
105: unwrap_used = "deny"
106: ```
107: 
108: ## rustfmt (formatação)
109: 
110: ```toml
111: # rustfmt.toml
112: max_width = 100
113: tab_spaces = 4
114: newline_style = "Unix"
115: use_small_heuristics = "Max"
116: ```
117: 
118: ```bash
119: cargo fmt                    # formatar
120: cargo fmt -- --check         # CI: verificar formatação
121: ```
122: 
123: ## Ferramentas essenciais do ecossistema
124: 
125: | Ferramenta | Instalação | Para que serve |
126: |------------|-----------|----------------|
127: | `cargo-edit` | built-in (`cargo add`) | Gerenciar dependências |
128: | `cargo-watch` | `cargo install cargo-watch` | Auto-compilar ao salvar |
129: | `cargo-tarpaulin` | `cargo install cargo-tarpaulin` | Cobertura de código |
130: | `cargo-audit` | `cargo install cargo-audit` | Auditoria de segurança |
131: | `cargo-deny` | `cargo install cargo-deny` | Licenças + segurança |
132: | `cargo-udeps` | `cargo install cargo-udeps` | Dependências não usadas |
133: | `cargo-expand` | `cargo install cargo-expand` | Expandir macros |
134: | `criterion` | `cargo add criterion --dev` | Benchmarks |
135: | `nextest` | `cargo install cargo-nextest` | Test runner mais rápido |
136: | `sccache` | `cargo install sccache` | Cache de compilação shared |
137: | `typos` | `cargo install typos-cli` | Spell checker |
138: | `cargo-machete` | `cargo install cargo-machete` | Deps não usadas |
139: | `cargo-outdated` | `cargo install cargo-outdated` | Deps desatualizadas |
140: 
141: ## CI básico (GitHub Actions)
142: 
143: ```yaml
144: name: CI
145: on: [push, pull_request]
146: jobs:
147:   ci:
148:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
149:     steps:
150:       - uses: actions/checkout@v4
151:       - uses: actions-rust-lang/setup-rust-toolchain@v1
152:       - run: cargo fmt -- --check
153:       - run: cargo clippy -- -D warnings
154:       - run: cargo test
155:       - run: cargo build --release
156: ```
157: 
158: ## Project Layout Padrão
159: 
160: ```
161: my_project/
162: ├── Cargo.toml          # manifesto
163: ├── Cargo.lock          # lockfile (commit!)
164: ├── rustfmt.toml        # opcional
165: ├── .cargo/
166: │   └── config.toml     # opcional
167: ├── src/
168: │   ├── main.rs         # entry point binário
169: │   ├── lib.rs          # entry point lib
170: │   └── bin/            # binários extras
171: │       └── other.rs
172: ├── tests/              # testes de integração
173: │   └── integration.rs
174: ├── benches/            # benchmarks
175: │   └── my_bench.rs
176: ├── examples/           # exemplos
177: │   └── example.rs
178: └── build.rs            # build script (opcional)
179: ```

(End of file - total 179 lines)
</content>
