<path>/projects/my/redmine-wrapper-rs/ai_guides/rust_guide/10-smart-pointers.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # 10 — Smart Pointers
2: 
3: ## Box<T> — Heap Allocation Simples
4: 
5: ```rust
6: // Aloca na heap
7: let b = Box::new(5);
8: assert_eq!(*b, 5);  // deref
9: 
10: // Tipos recursivos (precisam de tamanho conhecido)
11: enum List<T> {
12:     Cons(T, Box<List<T>>),
13:     Nil,
14: }
15: 
16: // Trait objects
17: let trait_obj: Box<dyn Iterator<Item = i32>> = Box::new(0..10);
18: ```
19: 
20: ## Deref Trait — Deref Coercion
21: 
22: ```rust
23: use std::ops::Deref;
24: 
25: struct MyBox<T>(T);
26: impl<T> Deref for MyBox<T> {
27:     type Target = T;
28:     fn deref(&self) -> &T { &self.0 }
29: }
30: 
31: // Deref coercion: &MyBox<String> → &String → &str
32: fn hello(name: &str) { println!("{name}"); }
33: let m = MyBox(String::from("Rust"));
34: hello(&m);  // automaticamente converte
35: ```
36: 
37: Deref coercion acontece com:
38: - `&T` → `&U` quando `T: Deref<Target=U>`
39: - `&mut T` → `&mut U` quando `T: DerefMut<Target=U>`
40: - `&mut T` → `&U` quando `T: Deref<Target=U>`
41: 
42: ## Drop Trait — Destructor
43: 
44: ```rust
45: struct CustomSmartPointer { data: String }
46: 
47: impl Drop for CustomSmartPointer {
48:     fn drop(&mut self) {
49:         println!("Dropping with data: {}", self.data);
50:     }
51: }
52: 
53: // Liberação manual (std::mem::drop)
54: let c = CustomSmartPointer { data: "stuff".into() };
55: drop(c);  // drop() da std, não Drop::drop
56: ```
57: 
58: ## Rc<T> — Reference Counting (non-threaded)
59: 
60: ```rust
61: use std::rc::Rc;
62: 
63: let a = Rc::new(5);
64: let b = Rc::clone(&a);  // incrementa contagem
65: let c = Rc::clone(&a);  // incrementa contagem
66: assert_eq!(Rc::strong_count(&a), 3);
67: 
68: // Precisa de Rc para múltiplos owners
69: // Não é Send nem Sync (só single-threaded)
70: 
71: // Cons list com Rc
72: enum List<T> {
73:     Cons(T, Rc<List<T>>),
74:     Nil,
75: }
76: use List::{Cons, Nil};
77: let a = Rc::new(Cons(5, Rc::new(Cons(10, Rc::new(Nil)))));
78: let b = Cons(3, Rc::clone(&a));
79: let c = Cons(4, Rc::clone(&a));
80: ```
81: 
82: ## Arc<T> — Atomic Reference Counting (threaded)
83: 
84: ```rust
85: use std::sync::Arc;
86: use std::thread;
87: 
88: let data = Arc::new(vec![1, 2, 3]);
89: 
90: let mut handles = vec![];
91: for _ in 0..3 {
92:     let data = Arc::clone(&data);
93:     handles.push(thread::spawn(move || {
94:         println!("{data:?}");
95:     }));
96: }
97: ```
98: 
99: ## RefCell<T> — Interior Mutability
100: 
101: Permite mutabilidade através de referência imutável — verificado em
102: **runtime** (não compile-time):
103: 
104: ```rust
105: use std::cell::RefCell;
106: 
107: let data = RefCell::new(5);
108: 
109: // borrow() — empresta imutável (runtime)
110: let borrowed = data.borrow();
111: // drop(borrowed);
112: 
113: // borrow_mut() — empresta mutável (runtime check)
114: *data.borrow_mut() += 1;
115: 
116: // Panic se violar regras de borrowing em runtime:
117: let a = data.borrow();       // OK
118: let b = data.borrow_mut();   // PANIC: already borrowed
119: ```
120: 
121: ### RefCell + Rc = Multiple Owners Mutável
122: 
123: ```rust
124: use std::rc::Rc;
125: use std::cell::RefCell;
126: 
127: let value = Rc::new(RefCell::new(5));
128: 
129: let a = Rc::clone(&value);
130: let b = Rc::clone(&value);
131: 
132: *a.borrow_mut() += 10;     // muta através de Rc
133: 
134: println!("{a:?}");  // 15
135: println!("{b:?}");  // 15
136: ```
137: 
138: ### Use Cases para RefCell
139: 
140: - Mock objects em testes
141: - Quando o compilador não entende que a mutação é segura
142: - Caches, contadores, estados internos
143: 
144: ## Weak<T> — Prevenindo Reference Cycles
145: 
146: ```rust
147: use std::rc::{Rc, Weak};
148: use std::cell::RefCell;
149: 
150: #[derive(Debug)]
151: struct Node {
152:     value: i32,
153:     parent: RefCell<Weak<Node>>,
154:     children: RefCell<Vec<Rc<Node>>>,
155: }
156: 
157: let leaf = Rc::new(Node {
158:     value: 3,
159:     parent: RefCell::new(Weak::new()),
160:     children: RefCell::new(vec![]),
161: });
162: 
163: let branch = Rc::new(Node {
164:     value: 5,
165:     parent: RefCell::new(Weak::new()),
166:     children: RefCell::new(vec![Rc::clone(&leaf)]),
167: });
168: 
169: *leaf.parent.borrow_mut() = Rc::downgrade(&branch);
170: 
171: // Weak::upgrade → Option<Rc<T>>
172: println!("parent: {:?}", leaf.parent.borrow().upgrade());
173: ```
174: 
175: | Rc methods | Weak methods |
176: |------------|--------------|
177: | `Rc::clone(&rc)` | `Rc::downgrade(&rc)` |
178: | `Rc::strong_count(&rc)` | `Weak::upgrade()` → `Option<Rc<T>>` |
179: | `Rc::weak_count(&rc)` | |
180: 
181: ## Cell<T> — Interior Mutability para Copy Types
182: 
183: ```rust
184: use std::cell::Cell;
185: 
186: let cell = Cell::new(5);
187: cell.set(10);
188: let val = cell.get();  // 10 (copia o valor)
189: // Sem runtime borrow check (Copy types são baratos de copiar)
190: ```
191: 
192: ## OnceCell / OnceLock — Inicialização Tardia
193: 
194: ```rust
195: use std::cell::OnceCell;
196: use std::sync::OnceLock;
197: 
198: static GLOBAL_CONFIG: OnceLock<Config> = OnceLock::new();
199: 
200: fn get_config() -> &'static Config {
201:     GLOBAL_CONFIG.get_or_init(|| {
202:         Config::load()
203:     })
204: }
205: ```
206: 
207: ## Resumo
208: 
209: | Smart Pointer | Multi-Owner | Mutável | Thread-Safe | Overhead |
210: |---------------|-------------|---------|-------------|----------|
211: | `Box<T>` | Não | Sim (se mut) | Sim | Mínimo |
212: | `Rc<T>` | Sim | Não | Não | Count (non-atomic) |
213: | `Arc<T>` | Sim | Não | Sim | Count (atomic) |
214: | `RefCell<T>` | Não | Sim (runtime) | Não | Runtime check |
215: | `Cell<T>` | Não | Sim (Copy) | Não | Nenhum |
216: | `Mutex<T>` | Não | Sim | Sim | Lock |
217: | `RwLock<T>` | Não | Sim | Sim | Lock |
218: | `OnceCell<T>` | 1x | Sim (1x) | Não (Cell) / Sim (Lock) | Option |
219: 
220: ## Boas Práticas
221: 
222: 1. **Box é o default** para heap allocation — use quando precisar
223: 2. **Rc/Arc** só quando múltiplos owners — caso contrário, mova
224: 3. **RefCell é code smell** em APIs públicas — prefira mut ownership
225: 4. **Arc<Mutex<T>>** é o padrão para estado compartilhado entre
226:    threads
227: 5. **Weak<T>** previne reference cycles — essencial em grafos/árvores
228: 6. **Cell para Copy types** — mais leve que RefCell
229: 7. **OnceCell para lazy statics** — melhor que `lazy_static!` ou
230:    `once_cell` (agora na std)

(End of file - total 230 lines)
</content>
```

**Title:** ai_guides/rust_guide/11-concurrency.md

  "filePath": "/projects/my/redmine-wrapper-rs/ai_guides/rust_guide/11-concurrency.md"
}
```

**Output:**
```
<path>/projects/my/redmine-wrapper-rs/ai_guides/rust_guide/11-concurrency.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # 11 — Concorrência (Threads, Message Passing, Shared State)
2: 
3: Rust garante **data-race freedom** em compile-time via sistema de
4: ownership + `Send`/`Sync` traits.
5: 
6: ## Threads
7: 
8: ```rust
9: use std::thread;
10: use std::time::Duration;
11: 
12: // Spawn
13: let handle = thread::spawn(|| {
14:     for i in 1..10 {
15:         println!("{i} from thread");
16:         thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(1));
17:     }
18: });
19: 
20: // join — espera thread terminar
21: handle.join().unwrap();
22: 
23: // move é necessário para capturar valores
24: let v = vec![1, 2, 3];
25: let handle = thread::spawn(move || {
26:     println!("{v:?}");
27: });
28: handle.join().unwrap();
29: ```
30: 
31: ### Builder Pattern
32: 
33: ```rust
34: use std::thread;
35: 
36: let builder = thread::Builder::new()
37:     .name("worker".into())
38:     .stack_size(1024 * 1024);
39: 
40: let handle = builder.spawn(|| {
41:     println!("named thread");
42: }).unwrap();
43: ```
44: 
45: ### Thread Pool (rayon)
46: 
47: ```rust
48: use rayon::prelude::*;
49: 
50: fn sum_of_squares(input: &[i32]) -> i32 {
51:     input.par_iter()       // parallel iterator
52:          .map(|&i| i * i)
53:          .sum()
54: }
55: 
56: // Parallel sort
57: let mut v = vec![...];
58: v.par_sort_unstable();
59: ```
60: 
61: ## Message Passing (Channels)
62: 
63: mpsc = Multiple Producer, Single Consumer:
64: 
65: ```rust
66: use std::sync::mpsc;
67: use std::thread;
68: 
69: let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel();
70: 
71: thread::spawn(move || {
72:     tx.send(42).unwrap();
73: });
74: 
75: let received = rx.recv().unwrap();     // blocking
76: // let received = rx.try_recv();        // non-blocking
77: 
78: // Múltiplos producers (clone tx)
79: let tx1 = tx.clone();
80: thread::spawn(move || { tx1.send(1).unwrap(); });
81: thread::spawn(move || { tx.send(2).unwrap(); });
82: 
83: // Iteração sobre receiver
84: for received in rx {
85:     println!("{received}");
86: }
87: ```
88: 
89: ### Async Channels (tokio / crossbeam)
90: 
91: ```rust
92: // tokio::sync::mpsc
93: use tokio::sync::mpsc;
94: let (tx, mut rx) = mpsc::channel(32);  // bounded
95: 
96: // crossbeam::channel — multi-consumer também
97: use crossbeam::channel;
98: let (tx, rx) = channel::unbounded();
99: ```
100: 
101: ## Shared State (Mutex + Arc)
102: 
103: ```rust
104: use std::sync::{Mutex, Arc};
105: use std::thread;
106: 
107: let counter = Arc::new(Mutex::new(0));
108: let mut handles = vec![];
109: 
110: for _ in 0..10 {
111:     let counter = Arc::clone(&counter);
112:     let handle = thread::spawn(move || {
113:         let mut num = counter.lock().unwrap();
114:         *num += 1;
115:     });
116:     handles.push(handle);
117: }
118: 
119: for handle in handles {
120:     handle.join().unwrap();
121: }
122: 
123: println!("{}", *counter.lock().unwrap()); // 10
124: ```
125: 
126: ### Mutex Internals
127: 
128: ```rust
129: {
130:     let mut data = mutex.lock().unwrap();
131:     *data += 1;
132:     // MutexGuard droppado aqui → unlock automático
133: }
134: // try_lock() para non-blocking
135: if let Some(mut guard) = mutex.try_lock().ok() {
136:     *guard += 1;
137: }
138: ```
139: 
140: ### RwLock (multiple readers / single writer)
141: 
142: ```rust
143: use std::sync::RwLock;
144: 
145: let lock = RwLock::new(5);
146: 
147: // Múltiplos readers simultâneos
148: let r1 = lock.read().unwrap();
149: let r2 = lock.read().unwrap();
150: 
151: // Writer (exclusivo)
152: let mut w = lock.write().unwrap();
153: *w += 2;
154: ```
155: 
156: ### Condvar (condition variable)
157: 
158: ```rust
159: use std::sync::{Mutex, Condvar};
160: let pair = Arc::new((Mutex::new(false), Condvar::new()));
161: let pair2 = Arc::clone(&pair);
162: 
163: thread::spawn(move || {
164:     let (lock, cvar) = &*pair2;
165:     let mut started = lock.lock().unwrap();
166:     *started = true;
167:     cvar.notify_one();  // acorda thread esperando
168: });
169: 
170: let (lock, cvar) = &*pair;
171: let mut started = lock.lock().unwrap();
172: while !*started {
173:     started = cvar.wait(started).unwrap();  // libera lock e espera
174: }
175: ```
176: 
177: ## Send e Sync (Marker Traits)
178: 
179: ```rust
180: // Send: pode ser transferido entre threads
181: // Sync: pode ser compartilhado entre threads (&T é Send)
182: 
183: // A maioria dos tipos são Send + Sync automaticamente
184: // Tipos raw pointer: !Send + !Sync
185: // Rc<T>: !Send + !Sync (contagem non-atomic)
186: // Arc<T>: Send + Sync
187: // RefCell<T>: Send + !Sync
188: // MutexGuard<'_, T>: !Send (deve ser droppado na mesma thread)
189: 
190: // Implementação manual (unsafe)
191: unsafe impl Send for MyType {}
192: unsafe impl Sync for MyType {}
193: ```
194: 
195: ## Barrier
196: 
197: ```rust
198: use std::sync::{Arc, Barrier};
199: use std::thread;
200: 
201: let barrier = Arc::new(Barrier::new(3));  // espera 3 threads
202: 
203: let mut handles = vec![];
204: for i in 0..3 {
205:     let barrier = Arc::clone(&barrier);
206:     handles.push(thread::spawn(move || {
207:         println!("thread {i} working");
208:         barrier.wait();       // todas esperam aqui
209:         println!("thread {i} past barrier");
210:     }));
211: }
212: ```
213: 
214: ## Atomic Types
215: 
216: ```rust
217: use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicU64, Ordering};
218: 
219: let counter = AtomicU64::new(0);
220: counter.fetch_add(1, Ordering::SeqCst);
221: 
222: // Ordering:
223: // Relaxed — sem garantias de ordenação (mais rápido)
224: // Release — writes visíveis para Acquire
225: // Acquire — vê writes de Release
226: // AcqRel — Acquire + Release
227: // SeqCst — sequencialmente consistente (default, mais lento)
228: ```
229: 
230: ## Boas Práticas
231: 
232: 1. **Prefira message passing** sobre shared state — mais seguro e
233:    testável
234: 2. **Arc<Mutex<T>>** é o padrão para shared state — mas evite lock
235:    contention
236: 3. **Lock scopes devem ser pequenos** — mutex guard vive até fim do
237:    escopo
238: 4. **Evite `unwrap()` em locks** — use `lock().expect("poisoned")`
239: 5. **Thread pools (rayon)** para tarefas paralelas genéricas
240: 6. **canal bounded (tokio)** para backpressure
241: 7. **Atomic types** são mais leves que Mutex para tipos simples
242: 8. **`Send`/`Sync`** são implementados automaticamente — raramente
243:    precisa implementar manualmente
244: 9. **Mutex poisoning** — se uma thread panica segurando o lock, o
245:    mutex fica corrompido. Use `.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())`
246: 10. **RwLock** para read-heavy workloads

(End of file - total 246 lines)
</content>
```

<path>/projects/my/redmine-wrapper-rs/ai_guides/rust_guide/14-unsafe-macros-ffi.md</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # 14 — Unsafe Rust, Macros e FFI
2: 
3: ## Unsafe Rust
4: 
5: Unsafe não desabilita o borrow checker — dá superpoderes adicionais:
6: 
7: ```rust
8: unsafe fn dangerous() {}
9: unsafe trait UnsafeTrait {}
10: unsafe impl UnsafeTrait for MyType {}
11: 
12: // 5 superpoderes:
13: ```
14: 
15: ### 1. Dereferenciar Raw Pointers
16: 
17: ```rust
18: let mut num = 5;
19: let r1 = &num as *const i32;         // raw pointer imutável
20: let r2 = &mut num as *mut i32;       // raw pointer mutável
21: 
22: unsafe {
23:     println!("r1: {}", *r1);          // deref
24:     *r2 = 10;
25: }
26: 
27: // Raw pointers podem ser nulos
28: let ptr: *const i32 = std::ptr::null();
29: ```
30: 
31: ### 2. Chamar Funções Unsafe (incluindo FFI)
32: 
33: ```rust
34: extern "C" {
35:     fn abs(input: i32) -> i32;       // libc
36: }
37: 
38: unsafe {
39:     println!("abs(-3) = {}", abs(-3));
40: }
41: ```
42: 
43: ### 3. Acessar/Modificar Static Mutáveis
44: 
45: ```rust
46: static mut COUNTER: u32 = 0;
47: 
48: fn add_to_count(inc: u32) {
49:     unsafe {
50:         COUNTER += inc;
51:     }
52: }
53: ```
54: 
55: ### 4. Implementar Unsafe Traits
56: 
57: ```rust
58: unsafe trait MyUnsafeTrait {}
59: 
60: unsafe impl MyUnsafeTrait for MyType {}
61: ```
62: 
63: ### 5. Acessar Campos de Union
64: 
65: ```rust
66: #[repr(C)]
67: union MyUnion {
68:     i: i32,
69:     f: f32,
70: }
71: 
72: let u = MyUnion { i: 42 };
73: unsafe {
74:     println!("{}.", u.i);
75: }
76: ```
77: 
78: ### Padrão Seguro sobre Unsafe
79: 
80: ```rust
81: use std::slice;
82: 
83: fn split_at_mut<T>(values: &mut [T], mid: usize) -> (&mut [T], &mut [T]) {
84:     let len = values.len();
85:     let ptr = values.as_mut_ptr();
86: 
87:     assert!(mid <= len);
88: 
89:     unsafe {
90:         (
91:             slice::from_raw_parts_mut(ptr, mid),
92:             slice::from_raw_parts_mut(ptr.add(mid), len - mid),
93:         )
94:     }
95: }
96: ```
97: 
98: ### Miri (UB Detector)
99: 
100: ```bash
101: cargo +nightly miri test    # detecta undefined behavior
102: ```
103: 
104: ## FFI (Foreign Function Interface)
105: 
106: ### Chamando C de Rust
107: 
108: ```rust
109: // Declarar funções externas
110: extern "C" {
111:     fn strlen(s: *const c_char) -> usize;
112: }
113: 
114: // Linking
115: #[link(name = "ssl")]
116: extern "C" { ... }
117: 
118: // Tipos compatíveis com C
119: #[repr(C)]
120: struct Point {
121:     x: f64,
122:     y: f64,
123: }
124: 
125: // Strings C
126: use std::ffi::{CStr, CString};
127: let c_str = CString::new("hello").unwrap();
128: unsafe { println!("{}", strlen(c_str.as_ptr())); }
129: ```
130: 
131: ### Exportando Rust para C
132: 
133: ```rust
134: #[no_mangle]
135: pub extern "C" fn call_from_c() -> i32 {
136:     println!("called from C");
137:     42
138: }
139: 
140: // Previne otimização (mingw / cdylib)
141: // Cargo.toml: crate-type = ["cdylib"]
142: ```
143: 
144: ### Bindgen (auto-generate bindings)
145: 
146: ```toml
147: [build-dependencies]
148: bindgen = "0.70"
149: ```
150: 
151: ```rust
152: // build.rs
153: fn main() {
154:     println!("cargo::rerun-if-changed=wrapper.h");
155:     let bindings = bindgen::Builder::default()
156:         .header("wrapper.h")
157:         .generate()
158:         .expect("Unable to generate bindings");
159: 
160:     bindings
161:         .write_to_file("src/bindings.rs")
162:         .expect("Couldn't write bindings");
163: }
164: ```
165: 
166: ## Macros
167: 
168: ### macro_rules! (declarative macros)
169: 
170: ```rust
171: // Macro simples
172: macro_rules! say_hello {
173:     () => {
174:         println!("Hello!");
175:     };
176: }
177: 
178: // Com argumentos
179: macro_rules! create_function {
180:     ($func_name:ident) => {
181:         fn $func_name() {
182:             println!("called {}", stringify!($func_name));
183:         }
184:     };
185: }
186: create_function!(foo);  // gera fn foo()
187: 
188: // Designators
189: // ident, expr, ty, pat, stmt, block, literal, tt, path, meta, vis
190: macro_rules! calculate {
191:     (eval $e:expr) => {{
192:         let val: usize = $e;
193:         println!("{} = {}", stringify!($e), val);
194:         val
195:     }};
196: }
197: 
198: // Repetition
199: macro_rules! vec {
200:     ( $( $x:expr ),* $(,)? ) => {
201:         {
202:             let mut temp_vec = Vec::new();
203:             $(
204:                 temp_vec.push($x);
205:             )*
206:             temp_vec
207:         }
208:     };
209: }
210: 
211: // Variadic
212: macro_rules! find_min {
213:     ($x:expr) => ($x);
214:     ($x:expr, $($y:expr),+) => (
215:         std::cmp::min($x, find_min!($($y),+))
216:     );
217: }
218: ```
219: 
220: ### Procedural Macros
221: 
222: ```rust
223: // Três tipos: custom derive, attribute-like, function-like
224: 
225: // Cargo.toml (macro crate)
226: [lib]
227: proc-macro = true
228: 
229: // custom_derive
230: #[proc_macro_derive(MyDerive)]
231: pub fn my_derive(input: TokenStream) -> TokenStream { ... }
232: 
233: // attribute
234: #[proc_macro_attribute]
235: pub fn log_call(_attr: TokenStream, item: TokenStream) -> TokenStream {
236:     println!("calling: {item}");
237:     item
238: }
239: 
240: // function-like
241: #[proc_macro]
242: pub fn sql(input: TokenStream) -> TokenStream { ... }
243: 
244: // Exemplo: derive macro básica
245: use proc_macro::TokenStream;
246: use quote::quote;
247: use syn::{parse_macro_input, DeriveInput};
248: 
249: #[proc_macro_derive(HelloMacro)]
250: pub fn hello_macro_derive(input: TokenStream) -> TokenStream {
251:     let ast = parse_macro_input!(input as DeriveInput);
252:     let name = &ast.ident;
253:     let gen = quote! {
254:         impl HelloMacro for #name {
255:             fn hello_macro() {
256:                 println!("Hello, Macro! My name is {}", stringify!(#name));
257:             }
258:         }
259:     };
260:     gen.into()
261: }
262: ```
263: 
264: ## Inline Assembly
265: 
266: ```rust
267: use std::arch::asm;
268: 
269: unsafe {
270:     asm!("nop");  // no-op instruction
271: 
272:     // Inputs, outputs, clobbers
273:     let x: u64 = 3;
274:     let y: u64;
275:     asm!("add {0}, {1}", inout(reg) x => y, in(reg) 5);
276: }
277: ```
278: 
279: ## Boas Práticas (Unsafe)
280: 
281: 1. **Encapsular unsafe em safe abstractions** — a função safe deve ser
282:    a API
283: 2. **Mínimo de código unsafe** — cada `unsafe { }` deve ser pequeno e
284:    justificado
285: 3. **Documentar invariantes** — // SAFETY: explica por que é safe
286: 4. **Rodar Miri em CI** se usar unsafe extensivamente
287: 5. **Preferir raw pointers a referências** em código unsafe (controle
288:    fino)
289: 6. **assert! invariantes** antes de código unsafe
290: 7. **Unsafe não desabilita borrow checker** — só dá poderes extras
291: 8. **Evitar transmute** — prefira conversões seguras
292: 9. **repr(C)** para compatibilidade com C
293: 10. **macro_rules! para código repetitivo**, proc macros para
294:     transformações complexas

(End of file - total 294 lines)
</content>
```

### 🤖 Assistant

💭 **Reasoning:**

<details>
<summary>Click to expand reasoning</summary>

Now I have a very thorough understanding of the entire project. Let me compile my analysis.

## Current State Analysis

### Project Overview
- **redmine-wrapper-rs**: A Rust library wrapping the Redmine REST API
- **22 resources** (issues, projects, users, etc.) — **86 endpoints**
- **Edition 2021** (not 2024)
- **Blocking** HTTP client (reqwest `blocking` feature)
- Uses `thiserror` for errors, `serde` for serialization, `uuid` v7 for correlation IDs
- Uses `std::sync::Mutex` for rate limiting
- Has a builder pattern for config
- 3 integration tests using wiremock + tokio runtime (mixed — uses tokio runtime just for wiremock, then blocking client)

### What the AI Guide says is "modern Rust" (Edition 2024 / 1.97)

Guide recommendations vs current state:

1. **Edition**: Guide says `edition = "2024"`, project uses `"2021"`
2. **rust-version**: Guide recommends `rust-version = "1.85"`, project doesn't have it
3. **Async**: Guide says tokio is the standard runtime, use async for I/O-bound work. Project uses blocking reqwest.
4. **Error handling**: Guide says `thiserror` for libraries (✓) but also suggests using `anyhow` in apps, and recommends `#[from]` and `#[source]` attributes (project already uses thiserror ✓).
5. **Logging**: Guide recommends `tracing` over `log`. Project uses `log`.
6. **HTTP**: Guide recommends `reqwest` (✓) but async version. Project uses blocking.
7. **Testing**: Guide recommends mock traits. Project uses wiremock with a fragile pattern (creating tokio runtime manually).
8. **Rate limiting**: Guide recommends `tokio::sync::Mutex` for async. Project uses `std::sync::Mutex` (OK for blocking but not ideal).
9. **Deref pattern**: Guide doesn't really recommend extensive `Deref` usage — `Deref` is meant for smart pointers, not for API convenience. Using `Deref` to access resources is a code smell.
10. **Module organization**: Guide recommends domain-oriented organization. Project is organized by technical layer (types, resources, http, core, client, utils). This is a reasonable structure for an API client library, but could be improved.
11. **Cargo.toml**: Guide recommends lints config, release profile optimization. Project lacks these.
12. **`#[must_use]`**: Project uses `#[must_use]` on all resource methods — but this doesn't make sense for `Result` return types (which already have `#[must_use]` built in). Only `get` methods returning non-Result values (like `Issue`) would benefit from it, but they return `Result` anyway.

### Key Issues / Gaps (detailed)

1. **Blocking HTTP**: `reqwest::blocking` is used. This is a significant limitation:
   - No async/await support
   - Thread blocking during HTTP calls
   - Can't integrate well with async frameworks (axum, actix)
   - `Mutex` usage is blocking (sleep in rate limiter blocks thread)

2. **Edition 2021 instead of 2024**: Missing out on:
   - `unsafe` precision improvements
   - `impl Trait` everywhere
   - `gen` blocks
   - Better lifetime elision
   - Better error messages

3. **Deref pattern for ResourceGroup**: `Deref` is used to make `RedmineClient` act like `ResourceGroup`. This is an anti-pattern — `Deref` should be for smart pointers, not for API convenience. It can cause confusion and unexpected behavior.

4. **Rate limiter**: The `SlidingWindow` uses `std::thread::sleep()` which blocks the entire thread. In an async context, this would be `tokio::time::sleep()`.

5. **`#[must_use]` on all methods**: Redundant for methods returning `Result` (which already has `#[must_use]`). Only `get_single`, `get`, etc. returning non-Result types would benefit.

6. **Builder pattern**: The `RedmineConfigBuilder` is reasonable but could use `#[derive(Default)]` more effectively. The `ResolvedConfig` / `RedmineConfig` separation is a bit over-engineered for what it does.

7. **No async testing**: Tests use `tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap()` and `rt.block_on()` to work with wiremock, which is async. This is a sign that the test infrastructure wants to be async but the library isn't.

8. **No `tracing`**: Uses `log` crate. `tracing` is more modern and structured, with spans and instrumentation support.

9. **Cargo.toml**: Missing `[lints]`, `[profile.release]` optimization, `rust-version`, `resolver = "2"` (implicit from edition 2021 but should be explicit).

10. **Clone for types**: The guide recommends `Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize` on all types. The project does this correctly (✓).

### What the user asked

The user asked me to:
1. Read the AI guide in detail
2. Analyze differences
3. Plan how to implement a more modern implementation using **async via axum/tokio** (what's simplest and integration-friendly)

They specifically mentioned "axum/tokio", which suggests they want the library to be async-first, and possibly to add axum as an integration layer or example.

### Modernization Plan

Let me think about what a realistic, incremental migration would look like:

**Phase 1: Infrastructure (Cargo.toml, Edition)**
- Update to Edition 2024
- Add `rust-version = "1.85"`
- Switch from `reqwest/blocking` to `reqwest` (async)
- Add `tokio` as dependency (not just dev)
- Add `[lints]` config
- Add release profile optimization
- Replace `log` + `env_logger` with `tracing` + `tracing-subscriber`

**Phase 2: Core async migration**
- Make `HttpClient` fully async (replace blocking calls with async)
- Replace `std::sync::Mutex<SlidingWindow>` with `tokio::sync::Mutex` or better yet, a lock-free approach
- Replace `std::thread::sleep()` with `tokio::time::sleep()` in rate limiter
- Make all resource methods `async fn`
- Remove `#[must_use]` from Result-returning methods (it's already implied)

**Phase 3: API refinement**
- Replace `Deref` with direct access methods or composition
- Simplify the config layer (maybe merge `RedmineConfig` and `ResolvedConfig`)
- Evaluate whether the Builder pattern is worth keeping or if a simpler `Config::new()` suffices

**Phase 4: Integration & examples**
- Add `axum` integration example
- Update `demo` and `check` examples to use async
- Update tests to be fully async (using `#[tokio::test]` instead of manual runtime)

Let me now structure this in a clear report for the user.

Wait, the user's original instruction says "use mainly async via axum/tokio". Let me think about what axum would be used for in an API client library...

Actually, looking at the AGENTS.md more carefully, this project is a *library* (an API wrapper). 25:     /// Retorna os detalhes de um anexo pelo ID.

34:     #[must_use]

36:         let path = format!("/attachments/{}.json", id);

53:     }

55:     /// Faz upload de um arquivo e retorna o token para uso posterior.
56:     ///
57:     /// O processo de upload ocorre em duas etapas:
58:     /// 1. Envio do conteúdo binário para `/uploads.json` — esta função executa esta etapa.
59:     /// 2. Uso do token retornado ao associar o anexo a uma issue (`CreateIssuePayload.uploads`)
60:     ///    ou a um arquivo de projeto (`CreateFilePayload`).
61:     ///
62:     /// # Parâmetros
63:     /// - `filename` — Nome do arquivo (usado como parâmetro de query)
64:     /// - `data` — Conteúdo binário do arquivo
65:     ///
66:     /// # Exemplo
67:     /// ```rust,ignore
68:     /// let token = client.attachments.upload("foto.png", &bytes)?;
69:     /// ```
70:     #[must_use]
71:     pub fn upload(&self, filename: &str, data: &[u8]) -> Result<String, RedmineError> {
72:         let path = format!("/uploads.json?filename={}", filename);
73:         let result: UploadTokenResponse = self.http.post_binary(&path, data, "application/octet-stream", "attachments.upload")?;
74:         Ok(result.upload.token)
75:     }
76: }
77: 
78: #[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
79: struct UploadTokenResponse {
80:     upload: UploadToken,
81: }

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # 3 — Ownership, Borrowing e Lifetimes
2: 
3: ## Ownership Rules
4: 
5: 1. Cada valor tem **exatamente um dono** (owner)
6: 2. O dono é a variável que o possui
7: 3. Quando o dono sai de escopo, o valor é **dropado** (drop)
8: 
9: ```rust
10: {                              // s não é válido aqui
11:     let s = String::from("olá"); // s é válido a partir daqui
12:     // usa s
13: }                              // escopo termina → s dropado
14: ```
15: 
16: ### Move vs Copy
17: 
18: ```rust
19: // Copy — tipos que ficam na stack
20: let x = 5;         // i32: Copy
21: let y = x;         // ainda posso usar x
22: 
23: // Move — tipos com heap allocation
24: let s1 = String::from("hello");
25: let s2 = s1;       // s1 é MOVED para s2
26: // println!("{s1}"); // ERROR: s1 foi movido!
27: 
28: // Clone — explícito, custoso
29: let s2 = s1.clone(); // deep copy
30: 
31: // Drop — tipos que não podem ser copiados
32: // Copy NÃO pode ser implementado com Drop
33: ```
34: 
35: ### Stack vs Heap
36: 
37: ```rust
38: // Stack: tamanho conhecido em compile-time
39: // i32 (4 bytes), bool (1 byte), [i32; 3] (12 bytes)
40: // (i32, f64) (12 bytes)
41: 
42: // Heap: tamanho dinâmico, alocado via allocator global
43: // String, Vec<T>, Box<T>, HashMap<K,V>
44: 
45: fn main() {
46:     let stack_i32: i32 = 42;               // Stack
47:     let heap_string: String = "hello".into(); // Stack (ptr, len, cap) → Heap (data)
48:     let heap_box: Box<i32> = Box::new(42);  // Stack (ptr) → Heap (i32)
49: }
50: ```

(Showing lines 1-50 of 218. Use offset=51 to continue.)
</content>
```

1: # 4 — Structs, Enums e Pattern Matching
2: 
3: ## Structs
4: 
5: ```rust
6: // Struct nomeada (C-like)
7: struct User {
8:     active: bool,
9:     username: String,
10:     sign_in_count: u64,
11: }
12: 
13: // Tuple struct (newtype pattern)
14: struct Color(i32, i32, i32);
15: struct Point(i32, i32, i32);
16: 
17: // Unit struct (zero-size)
18: struct AlwaysEqual;
19: ```
20: 
21: ### Instanciação e shorthand
22: 
23: ```rust
24: let user = User {
25:     active: true,
26:     username: String::from("alice"),
27:     sign_in_count: 1,
28: };
29: 
30: // Field init shorthand (variável com mesmo nome do campo)
31: let username = String::from("bob");
32: let user = User {
33:     username,  // equivalente a username: username
34:     ..Default::default()  // update syntax
35: };
36: 
37: // Destructuring
38: let User { username, .. } = user;
39: ```
40: 
41: ### #[derive] — traits comuns
42: 
43: ```rust
44: #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Default)]
45: struct Config {
46:     host: String,
47:     port: u16,
48: }
49: ```
50:

Aqui está a revisão completa.

---

# Revisão Arquitetural e Plano de Modernização

## 1. Diagnóstico — O Projeto Hoje

### Pontos Fortes (mantenha)

| Aspecto | Status |
|---------|--------|
| **Cobertura de API** | 22 resources, 86 endpoints — completa |
| **Tipos serde** | `Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize` em todos (✓ alinhado com o guia) |
| **Error handling** | `thiserror` + enum `RedmineError` (✓ padrão correto para bibliotecas) |
| **Correlation IDs** | UUID v7 em erros de API (✓ rastreabilidade) |
| **Documentação** | Docs em português, exemplos nos métodos |
| **Testes de integração** | wiremock cobre cenários críticos (auth, 404, 422) |
| **Rate limiting** | Sliding window funcional |
| **MPL-2.0** | Licença clara |

### Pontos Fracos (precisa mudar)

| GAP | Onde | Impacto |
|-----|------|---------|
| **Blocking HTTP** | `reqwest::blocking` em todo `HttpClient` | Não compõe com async; trava threads |
| **Edition 2021** | `Cargo.toml` | Perde melhorias de 2024 (impl Trait, gen blocks, unsafe) |
| **`Deref` para composição** | `RedmineClient` → `ResourceGroup` | Anti-pattern; `Deref` é para smart pointers |
| **`#[must_use]` redundante** | Todos os métodos resource | `Result` já é `#[must_use]`; só polui o código |
| **`std::thread::sleep`** | `rate_limiter.rs:55` | Bloqueia thread inteira; incompatível com async |
| **`std::sync::Mutex`** | `HttpClient` contém `Mutex<SlidingWindow>` | Bloqueante; em async deve ser `tokio::sync::Mutex` |
| **`log` crate** | Em todo o projeto | `tracing` é o padrão moderno (spans, structured fields) |
| **Builder + ResolvedConfig** | `config.rs` | Duas structs para pouca diferença; pode simplificar |
| **Testes híbridos** | `client_test.rs` cria runtime tokio manualmente | Frágil; deveria usar `#[tokio::test]` |
| **Sem `tracing`** | — | Perde instrumentação automática |
| **Sem `[lints]`** | `Cargo.toml` | Sem governance de qualidade |
| **Sem `[profile.release]`** | `Cargo.toml` | Release binário não otimizado |
| **Clone nos Wrappers** | `CreateIssueWrapper`, `UpdateIssueWrapper` | `payload.clone()` desnecessário; podia ser referência |

---

## 2. Mapeamento Guia → Projeto — Diferenças Detalhadas

| Seção do Guia | Recomendação | Projeto Atual | Ação |
|---------------|--------------|---------------|------|
| `00-intro.md` | Edition 2024, rust-version 1.85 | Edition 2021 | ⚡ Migrar |
| `01-toolchain.md` | `[lints]`, `[profile.release]`, CI | Ausente | ➕ Adicionar |
| `06-error-handling.md` | `thiserror` para libs | ✅ Usa | Manter |
| `12-async.md` | Tokio runtime, async I/O | Blocking reqwest | 🔄 Migrar |
| `13-testing.md` | `#[tokio::test]`, mock traits | Manual `rt.block_on` | 🔄 Simplificar |
| `15-cargo-deep.md` | `resolver = "2"`, workspace lints | Não declarado | ➕ Adicionar |
| `16-project-practical.md` | `tracing` > `log`, lib+thin binary | `log` usado | 🔄 Migrar |

---

## 3. Plano de Modernização — 4 Fases Incrementais

Cada fase == 1-2 semanas de trabalho, testável independentemente.

### Fase 1: Infraestrutura (Cargo.toml + toolchain)

**Objetivo**: Atualizar a base do projeto sem mudar comportamento.

```
Cargo.toml
├── edition = "2024"
├── rust-version = "1.85"
├── [lints] rust/clippy
├── [profile.release] otimizado
└── dependências atualizadas
```

**Mudanças concretas:**

```toml
# Antes
edition = "2021"

# Depois
edition = "2024"
rust-version = "1.85"

[lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "forbid"
missing_docs = "warn"

[lints.clippy]
pedantic = "warn"
unwrap_used = "deny"

[profile.release]
opt-level = 3
lto = "fat"
codegen-units = 1
strip = "symbols"
panic = "abort"
```

**Dependências**: Substituir `log` por `tracing`:

```toml
# Antes
log = "0.4"

# Depois
tracing = "0.1"
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"], optional = true }
```

**Testes**: `cargo test` continua passando sem mudança de lógica.

---

### Fase 2: Async Core (HttpClient + RateLimiter)

**Objetivo**: Tornar toda a camada HTTP assíncrona.

**Mudanças no `Cargo.toml`:**

```toml
# Antes
reqwest = { version = "0.12", default-features = false, features = ["json", "blocking"] }

# Depois
reqwest = { version = "0.12", default-features = false, features = ["json"] }
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["time", "sync"] }
```

**Mudanças no `HttpClient` (`src/http/client.rs`):**

```rust
// ANTES — blocking
pub fn get<T: DeserializeOwned>(&self, path: &str, query: &[(&str, String)], operation: &str) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {
    let url = self.build_url(path, query)?;
    self.execute(|| self.client.get(&url).headers(self.auth_headers()), operation)
}

// DEPOIS — async
pub async fn get<T: DeserializeOwned>(&self, path: &str, query: &[(&str, String)], operation: &str) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {
    let url = self.build_url(path, query)?;
    self.execute(|| async {
        self.client.get(&url).headers(self.auth_headers()).send().await
    }, operation).await
}
```

**Rate limiter** — de `std::thread::sleep` para `tokio::time::sleep`:

```rust
// ANTES
use std::thread::sleep;
fn acquire(&mut self) { /* ... */ std::thread::sleep(duration); /* ... */ }

// DEPOIS
use tokio::time::sleep;
async fn acquire(&mut self) { /* ... */ sleep(duration).await; /* ... */ }
```

E o `Mutex<SlidingWindow>` vira `tokio::sync::Mutex<SlidingWindow>`:

```rust
// ANTES
rate_limiter: Mutex<SlidingWindow>,

// DEPOIS
rate_limiter: tokio::sync::Mutex<SlidingWindow>,
```

**Mudanças nos Resources** — todos os métodos viram `async fn`:

```rust
// ANTES (issues.rs)
pub fn list(&self, filter: Option<&IssueFilter>) -> Result<Vec<Issue>, RedmineError> {
    // blocking call
}

// DEPOIS
pub async fn list(&self, filter: Option<&IssueFilter>) -> Result<Vec<Issue>, RedmineError> {
    // await
}
```

**Padrão de implementação — mínimo diff por resource:**

```rust
impl IssuesResource {
    pub(crate) fn new(http: Arc<HttpClient>) -> Self { Self { http } }
    
    pub async fn list(&self, filter: Option<&IssueFilter>) -> Result<Vec<Issue>, RedmineError> {
        let query = /* build query */;
        self.http.get_all_paginated("/issues.json", "issues", &query, "issues.list").await
    }
    
    pub async fn get(&self, id: RedmineId) -> Result<Issue, RedmineError> {
        let path = format!("/issues/{}.json", id);
        self.http.get_single(&path, "issue", &[], "issues.get").await
    }
    // ... etc
}
```

**Testes** — de runtime manual para `#[tokio::test]`:

```rust
// ANTES
#[test]
fn test_issues_list() {
    let (server, client) = setup();
    let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new().unwrap();
    rt.block_on(async { /* mock setup */ });
    let result = client.issues.list(None).unwrap();
}

// DEPOIS
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_issues_list() {
    let (server, client) = setup().await;
    // mock setup (já está em contexto async)
    let result = client.issues.list(None).await.unwrap();
}
```

---

### Fase 3: Refinamento da API Pública

**3a. Eliminar `Deref` para `ResourceGroup`**

```rust
// ANTES — RedmineClient faz Deref para ResourceGroup
impl Deref for RedmineClient {
    type Target = ResourceGroup;
    fn deref(&self) -> &Self::Target { &self.inner }
}
// Uso: client.issues.list(None)?

// DEPOIS — acesso direto via campo
pub struct RedmineClient {
    pub config: ResolvedConfig,
    pub issues: IssuesResource,
    pub projects: ProjectsResource,
    // ... todos os 22 resources como campos públicos
}
// Uso: client.issues.list(None).await?
```

Isso elimina uma camada de indireção, torna a struct mais explícita e evita o anti-pattern `Deref`.

**3b. Simplificar config layer**

Fundir `RedmineConfig` + `ResolvedConfig` num só tipo, com `Default` implementado:

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct RedmineConfig {
    pub base_url: String,
    pub token: Option<String>,
    pub switch_user: Option<String>,
    pub timeout: Duration,
    pub max_rps: u32,
}

impl Default for RedmineConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            base_url: String::new(),
            token: None,
            switch_user: None,
            timeout: Duration::from_secs(DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECS),
            max_rps: DEFAULT_MAX_RPS,
        }
    }
}
```

Remover o Builder — para este nível de simplicidade, `RedmineConfig { base_url: "...", ..Default::default() }` é suficiente. Se quiser manter builder para fluência, que seja simples:

```rust
impl RedmineConfig {
    pub fn builder() -> RedmineConfigBuilder { RedmineConfigBuilder::default() }
}
```

**3c. Remover `#[must_use]` redundante**

`Result<T, RedmineError>` já é `#[must_use]` por padrão. Remover de todos os métodos que retornam `Result`. Manter apenas em construtores que retornam `Self` (não `Result`).

**3d. Eliminar `payload.clone()` nos wrappers**

Os `*Wrapper` clones o payload desnecessariamente. Usar referência:

```rust
// ANTES
pub(crate) struct CreateIssueWrapper {
    pub issue: CreateIssuePayload,  // toma ownership
}
// uso: self.http.post_single("/issues.json", "issue", &CreateIssueWrapper { issue: payload.clone() }, ...)

// DEPOIS
pub(crate) struct CreateIssueWrapper<'a> {
    pub issue: &'a CreateIssuePayload,
}
// uso: self.http.post_single("/issues.json", "issue", &CreateIssueWrapper { issue: payload }, ...).await
```

---

### Fase 4: Integração + Exemplos + Axum

**4a. Exemplo axum** — como consumir a biblioteca de dentro de um handler axum:

```rust
// examples/axum_server.rs
use axum::{extract::State, routing::get, Json, Router};
use redmine_wrapper::{RedmineClient, RedmineConfig};

async fn list_issues(State(client): State<RedmineClient>) -> Json<serde_json::Value> {
    match client.issues.list(None).await {
        Ok(issues) => Json(serde_json::json!({ "issues": issues })),
        Err(e) => Json(serde_json::json!({ "error": e.to_string() })),
    }
}

#[tokio::main]
async fn main() {
    let client = RedmineClient::new(
        RedmineConfig { base_url: std::env::var("REDMINE_URL").unwrap(), ..Default::default() }
    ).unwrap();

    let app = Router::new()
        .route("/issues", get(list_issues))
        .with_state(client);

    let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind("0.0.0.0:3000").await.unwrap();
    axum::serve(listener, app).await.unwrap();
}
```

**4b. `Send + Sync` no `RedmineClient`**

Como arcabouço axum exige `State` `Send + Sync`, garantir que `HttpClient` (agora async) implemente ambos. Com `tokio::sync::Mutex` + `Arc`, isso é automático.

**4c. Instrumentação com `tracing`**

```rust
use tracing::{info, instrument};

impl IssuesResource {
    #[instrument(skip(self), fields(operation = "issues.list"))]
    pub async fn list(&self, filter: Option<&IssueFilter>) -> Result<Vec<Issue>, RedmineError> {
        // ...
    }
}
```

---

## 4. Roadmap Resumido

```
Fase 1: Infra      [1 semana]
├── Cargo.toml: edition 2024, lints, profile
├── log → tracing
└── cargo clippy --fix

Fase 2: Async Core [2 semanas] ← mais crítica
├── reqwest blocking → async
├── Mutex → tokio::sync::Mutex
├── thread::sleep → tokio::time::sleep
├── Todos resources: fn → async fn
├── HttpClient: métodos async
└── Testes: #[tokio::test]

Fase 3: Refinamento [1 semana]
├── Eliminar Deref
├── Simplificar config layer
├── Remover #[must_use] redundante
└── Otimizar clones nos wrappers

Fase 4: Integração  [1 semana]
├── Exemplo axum server
├── Instrumentação tracing
├── Demo atualizado para .await
└── Check atualizado para .await
```

**Total estimado: ~5 semanas** para um projeto que já está funcional e testado.

---

## 5. Decisões Arquiteturais — Trade-offs

| Decisão | Opções | Escolha | Rationale |
|---------|--------|---------|-----------|
| Runtime async | tokio / async-std / smol | **tokio** | Padrão da indústria, ecossistema mais rico (axum, reqwest, sqlx), guia recomenda |
| HTTP client | reqwest / hyper / ureq | **reqwest** (async) | Já está no projeto, só trocar feature |
| Logging | tracing / log | **tracing** | Structured, spans, integração com axum, padrão moderno |
| Config layer | Builder / struct direta | **struct + Default** | Simplicidade > padrões; builder só se houver validação complexa |
| Acesso resources | Deref / campos diretos | **campos diretos** | `Deref` é code smell para composição; campos são explícitos |
| Rate limiter | Mutex sync/async / lock-free | **tokio::sync::Mutex** | Simples, correto para async; lock-free é premature optimization |

---

## 6. Riscos e Mitigações

| Risco | Mitigação |
|-------|-----------|
| **Breaking change para consumidores** | Marcar como v0.2.0, documentar migration guide (`fn` → `async fn`, `.await`) |
| **Testes existentes quebram** | Migrar incrementalmente: primeiro async core, depois ajustar testes um a um |
| **Performance de mutex async** | `tokio::sync::Mutex` é leve; se rate limiter for bottleneck, migrar para `tokio::sync::Semaphore` |
| **Edition 2024 pode exigir rustc mais novo** | `rust-version = "1.85"` é MSRV correspondente |
| **Complexidade dos Wrappers** | Simplificar com referências em vez de clones — testar borrow checker |

---

## 7. Conclusão

O projeto `redmine-wrapper-rs` está **funcional e bem estruturado** para os padrões de 2021. A migração para async + Edition 2024 é **totalmente viável e incremental**, seguindo o padrão **Strangler Fig** (que o próprio AGENTS.md recomenda): cada fase substitui um pedaço sem reescrever do zero.

O maior ganho está na **Fase 2 (Async Core)**: uma vez que `HttpClient` seja async, todo o resto flui naturalmente — os resources viram `async fn`, os testes usam `#[tokio::test]`, e o ecossistema (axum, tokio, tracing) se integra sem atrito.

Quer que eu comece a implementar? Posso sugerir começar pela Fase 1 (Cargo.toml + lints + tracing) que é a de menor risco.

{
  "filePath": "/projects/my/redmine-wrapper-rs/src/http/rate_limiter.rs",
  "content": "// This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n// License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n// file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.\n\nuse std::collections::VecDeque;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\n\n/// Implementação de sliding window para controle de taxa de requisições.\n///\n/// Permite no máximo `max_requests` em uma janela de tempo (1 segundo).\n/// Bloqueia a task se o limite for atingido, aguardando até que\n/// uma posição seja liberada.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct SlidingWindow {\n    /// Máximo de requisições permitidas na janela.\n    max_requests: u32,\n\n    /// Duração da janela deslizante.\n    window: Duration,\n\n    /// Timestamps das requisições recentes.\n    timestamps: VecDeque<Instant>,\n}\n\nimpl SlidingWindow {\n    /// Cria uma nova janela deslizante.\n    pub fn new(max_requests: u32) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            max_requests,\n            window: Duration::from_secs(1),\n            timestamps: VecDeque::with_capacity(max_requests as usize),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Aguarda até que uma requisição possa ser feita, respeitando o limite.\n    ///\n    /// Remove timestamps expirados e bloqueia a task atual se necessário.\n    pub async fn acquire(&mut self) {\n        let now = Instant::now();\n        let cutoff = now - self.window;\n\n        // Remove timestamps expirados\n        while let Some(&ts) = self.timestamps.front() {\n            if ts < cutoff {\n                self.timestamps.pop_front();\n            } else {\n                break;\n            }\n        }\n\n        if self.timestamps.len() >= self.max_requests as usize {\n            // Aguarda até o timestamp mais antigo expirar\n            if let Some(&oldest) = self.timestamps.front() {\n                let sleep_duration = cutoff - oldest + self.window;\n                tokio::time::sleep(sleep_duration).await;\n            }\n            // Após dormir, remove o timestamp mais antigo\n            self.timestamps.pop_front();\n        }\n\n        self.timestamps.push_back(now);\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_allows_requests_under_limit() {\n        let mut limiter = SlidingWindow::new(10);\n        limiter.acquire().await;\n        limiter.acquire().await;\n        limiter.acquire().await;\n        limiter.acquire().await;\n        assert_eq!(limiter.timestamps.len(), 4);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_evicts_expired_timestamps() {\n        let mut limiter = SlidingWindow::new(5);\n        let past = Instant::now() - Duration::from_secs(2);\n        for _ in 0..5 {\n            limiter.timestamps.push_back(past);\n        }\n        limiter.acquire().await;\n        assert_eq!(limiter.timestamps.len(), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_blocks_when_over_limit() {\n        let mut limiter = SlidingWindow::new(2);\n        limiter.acquire().await;\n        limiter.acquire().await;\n        let start = Instant::now();\n        limiter.acquire().await;\n        // Deve ter bloqueado brevemente\n        assert!(start.elapsed() < Duration::from_secs(2));\n    }\n}\n"
}
```

**Input:**
```json
{
  "filePath": "/projects/my/redmine-wrapper-rs/src/http/client.rs",
  "content": "// This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n// License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n// file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.\n\nuse std::future::Future;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\n\nuse reqwest::{Client, Response};\nuse reqwest::header::{HeaderMap, HeaderValue, CONTENT_TYPE};\nuse serde::de::DeserializeOwned;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse tokio::sync::Mutex;\nuse url::form_urlencoded::Serializer as UrlSerializer;\n\nuse crate::core::config::ResolvedConfig;\nuse crate::core::constants::DEFAULT_PAGINATION_LIMIT;\nuse crate::core::errors::{ErrorCategory, ErrorContext, RedmineError};\nuse crate::http::pagination::PaginationParams;\nuse crate::http::rate_limiter::SlidingWindow;\n\nconst USER_AGENT: &str = concat!(\"redmine-wrapper-rs/\", env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\"));\n\n/// Cliente HTTP interno que gerencia autenticação, rate limiting e parsing.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub(crate) struct HttpClient {\n    client: Client,\n    config: ResolvedConfig,\n    rate_limiter: Mutex<SlidingWindow>,\n}\n\nimpl HttpClient {\n    /// Cria um novo HttpClient a partir da config resolvida.\n    pub fn new(config: ResolvedConfig) -> Result<Self, RedmineError> {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        headers.insert(CONTENT_TYPE, HeaderValue::from_static(\"application/json\"));\n\n        let client = Client::builder()\n            .default_headers(headers)\n            .timeout(config.timeout)\n            .user_agent(USER_AGENT)\n            .build()\n            .map_err(|e| RedmineError::Config(format!(\"falha ao criar HTTP client: {e}\")))?;\n\n        Ok(Self {\n            client,\n            rate_limiter: Mutex::new(SlidingWindow::new(config.max_rps)),\n            config,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma requisição GET e desserializa a resposta JSON.\n    pub async fn get<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        query: &[(&str, String)],\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let url = self.build_url(path, query)?;\n        self.execute(\n            || async {\n                self.client\n                    .get(&url)\n                    .headers(self.auth_headers())\n                    .send()\n                    .await\n            },\n            operation,\n        )\n        .await\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma requisição POST com corpo JSON e desserializa a resposta.\n    pub async fn post<T: DeserializeOwned, B: Serialize>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        body: &B,\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let url = self.build_url(path, &[])?;\n        let json_body = serde_json::to_string(body)?;\n        let body_clone = json_body.clone();\n        self.execute(\n            || async {\n                self.client\n                    .post(&url)\n                    .headers(self.auth_headers())\n                    .body(body_clone)\n                    .send()\n                    .await\n            },\n            operation,\n        )\n        .await\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma requisição POST com dados binários (upload de arquivos).\n    pub async fn post_binary<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        data: &[u8],\n        content_type: &str,\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let url = self.build_url(path, &[])?;\n        let body = data.to_vec();\n        let ct = content_type.to_string();\n        self.execute(\n            || async {\n                self.client\n                    .post(&url)\n                    .headers(self.auth_headers())\n                    .header(CONTENT_TYPE, ct.as_str())\n                    .body(body.clone())\n                    .send()\n                    .await\n            },\n            operation,\n        )\n        .await\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma requisição PUT com corpo JSON para atualizar um recurso.\n    pub async fn put<T: DeserializeOwned, B: Serialize>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        body: &B,\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let url = self.build_url(path, &[])?;\n        let json_body = serde_json::to_string(body)?;\n        let body_clone = json_body.clone();\n        self.execute(\n            || async {\n                self.client\n                    .put(&url)\n                    .headers(self.auth_headers())\n                    .body(body_clone)\n                    .send()\n                    .await\n            },\n            operation,\n        )\n        .await\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma GET e extrai um campo específico do JSON de resposta.\n    pub async fn get_single<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        key: &str,\n        query: &[(&str, String)],\n        op: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let v: serde_json::Value = self.get(path, query, op).await?;\n        let inner = v.get(key).ok_or_else(|| {\n            RedmineError::api(\n                ErrorCategory::ParseError,\n                200,\n                format!(\"campo '{key}' não encontrado\"),\n                ErrorContext {\n                    operation: Some(op.into()),\n                    ..Default::default()\n                },\n            )\n        })?;\n        serde_json::from_value(inner.clone()).map_err(RedmineError::from)\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma POST e extrai um campo específico do JSON de resposta.\n    pub async fn post_single<T: DeserializeOwned, B: Serialize>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        key: &str,\n        body: &B,\n        op: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let v: serde_json::Value = self.post(path, body, op).await?;\n        let inner = v.get(key).ok_or_else(|| {\n            RedmineError::api(\n                ErrorCategory::ParseError,\n                201,\n                format!(\"campo '{key}' não encontrado\"),\n                ErrorContext {\n                    operation: Some(op.into()),\n                    ..Default::default()\n                },\n            )\n        })?;\n        serde_json::from_value(inner.clone()).map_err(RedmineError::from)\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma GET com suporte a paginação e retorna os itens da página\n    /// juntamente com o total de registros disponíveis.\n    pub async fn get_paginated<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        item_key: &str,\n        params: Option<&PaginationParams>,\n        query: &[(&str, String)],\n        op: &str,\n    ) -> Result<(Vec<T>, u32), RedmineError> {\n        let mut q = query.to_vec();\n        if let Some(p) = params {\n            for (k, v) in p.to_query() {\n                q.push((k, v));\n            }\n        } else {\n            q.push((\"limit\", DEFAULT_PAGINATION_LIMIT.to_string()));\n        }\n        let v: serde_json::Value = self.get(path, &q, op).await?;\n        let total = v\n            .get(\"total_count\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n            .unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n        let items = v\n            .get(item_key)\n            .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n        Ok((items, total))\n    }\n\n    /// Executa requisições GET paginadas automaticamente até obter todos os\n    /// registros.\n    pub async fn get_all_paginated<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        item_key: &str,\n        query: &[(&str, String)],\n        op: &str,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<T>, RedmineError> {\n        let limit = DEFAULT_PAGINATION_LIMIT;\n        let mut offset = 0u32;\n        let mut all = Vec::new();\n        loop {\n            let mut pq = query.to_vec();\n            pq.push((\"offset\", offset.to_string()));\n            pq.push((\"limit\", limit.to_string()));\n            let v: serde_json::Value = self.get(path, &pq, op).await?;\n            let total = v\n                .get(\"total_count\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n                .unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n            let items: Vec<T> = v\n                .get(item_key)\n                .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n                .unwrap_or_default();\n            let c = items.len() as u32;\n            all.extend(items);\n            if offset + c >= total {\n                break;\n            }\n            offset += limit;\n        }\n        Ok(all)\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma requisição DELETE para remover um recurso.\n    pub async fn delete(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        query: &[(&str, String)],\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<(), RedmineError> {\n        let url = self.build_url(path, query)?;\n        self.execute_void(\n            || async {\n                self.client\n                    .delete(&url)\n                    .headers(self.auth_headers())\n                    .send()\n                    .await\n            },\n            operation,\n        )\n        .await\n    }\n\n    fn auth_headers(&self) -> HeaderMap {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        if let Some(ref token) = self.config.token {\n            headers.insert(\n                \"X-Redmine-API-Key\",\n                HeaderValue::from_str(token).unwrap(),\n            );\n        }\n        if let Some(ref switch_user) = self.config.switch_user {\n            headers.insert(\n                \"X-Redmine-Switch-User\",\n                HeaderValue::from_str(switch_user).unwrap(),\n            );\n        }\n        headers\n    }\n\n    fn build_url(&self, path: &str, query: &[(&str, String)]) -> Result<String, RedmineError> {\n        let mut url = self.config.api_url(path);\n        if !query.is_empty() {\n            url.push('?');\n            let mut serializer = UrlSerializer::new(&mut url);\n            for (k, v) in query {\n                serializer.append_pair(k, v);\n            }\n            serializer.finish();\n        }\n        Ok(url)\n    }\n\n    async fn execute<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        &self,\n        request_fn: impl Fn() -> Fut,\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError>\n    where\n        Fut: Future<Output = Result<Response, reqwest::Error>>,\n    {\n        self.acquire_rate_limit().await;\n\n        let response = request_fn().await.map_err(|e| {\n            if e.is_timeout() {\n                RedmineError::Timeout {\n                    duration: self.config.timeout,\n                    context: Box::new(ErrorContext {\n                        operation: Some(operation.into()),\n                        ..Default::default()\n                    }),\n                }\n            } else {\n                RedmineError::Http(e)\n            }\n        })?;\n\n        Self::handle_response(response, operation)\n    }\n\n    async fn execute_void(\n        &self,\n        request_fn: impl Fn() -> Fut,\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<(), RedmineError>\n    where\n        Fut: Future<Output = Result<Response, reqwest::Error>>,\n    {\n        self.acquire_rate_limit().await;\n\n        let response = request_fn().await.map_err(|e| {\n            if e.is_timeout() {\n                RedmineError::Timeout {\n                    duration: self.config.timeout,\n                    context: Box::new(ErrorContext {\n                        operation: Some(operation.into()),\n                        ..Default::default()\n                    }),\n                }\n            } else {\n                RedmineError::Http(e)\n            }\n        })?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n        if status.is_success() {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        let body = response.text().await.unwrap_or_default();\n        Err(Self::map_error(status.as_u16(), &body, operation))\n    }\n\n    fn handle_response<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        response: Response,\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let status = response.status();\n\n        if status == 204 {\n            return Err(RedmineError::api(\n                ErrorCategory::ParseError,\n                204,\n                \"resposta 204 sem corpo\",\n                ErrorContext {\n                    operation: Some(operation.into()),\n                    http_status: Some(204),\n                    ..Default::default()\n                },\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let body = futures::executor::block_on(response.text()).unwrap_or_default();\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            return Err(Self::map_error(status.as_u16(), &body, operation));\n        }\n\n        serde_json::from_str(&body).map_err(|e| {\n            tracing::error!(\n                target: \"redmine_wrapper::http\",\n                \"{}: falha ao desserializar resposta: {}\",\n                operation,\n                e,\n            );\n            let ctx = ErrorContext {\n                operation: Some(operation.into()),\n                http_status: Some(status.as_u16()),\n                response_body: Some(body),\n                ..Default::default()\n            };\n            RedmineError::api(ErrorCategory::ParseError, status.as_u16(), e.to_string(), ctx)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn map_error(status: u16, body: &str, operation: &str) -> RedmineError {\n        let category = ErrorCategory::from_status(status).unwrap_or(ErrorCategory::InternalError);\n\n        let api_errors: Option<Vec<String>> = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(body)\n            .ok()\n            .and_then(|v| {\n                v.get(\"errors\")\n                    .and_then(|e| serde_json::from_value(e.clone()).ok())\n            });\n\n        let detail = api_errors\n            .as_ref()\n            .and_then(|e| e.first().cloned())\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| match category {\n                ErrorCategory::RateLimited => \"limite de taxa excedido\".into(),\n                ErrorCategory::ImpersonationFailed => \"impersonação inválida\".into(),\n                _ => format!(\"erro HTTP {status}\"),\n            });\n\n        let context = ErrorContext {\n            operation: Some(operation.into()),\n            http_status: Some(status),\n            api_errors,\n            response_body: Some(body.into()),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n\n        if category == ErrorCategory::RateLimited {\n            let retry_after = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(body)\n                .ok()\n                .and_then(|v| v.get(\"retry_after\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()));\n            return RedmineError::RateLimited {\n                retry_after,\n                context: Box::new(context),\n            };\n        }\n\n        RedmineError::api(category, status, detail, context)\n    }\n\n    async fn acquire_rate_limit(&self) {\n        let mut limiter = self.rate_limiter.lock().await;\n        limiter.acquire().await;\n    }\n}\n"
}

The closure `|| { async { ... } }` works differently from a non-async closure. The `async { ... }` block doesn't execute immediately - it returns a Future. So the closure returns a Future, and the captures of the closure are available for the Future to borrow.

When we write:

Here, `f` captures `s` by reference (since `Fn` requires shared ref). When `f()` is called, it creates an async block that borrows `s`. The async block returns a Future that, when polled, prints `s`.

But for `Fn`, the closure needs to be callable multiple times. If the async block moves `s`, then the second call wouldn't work because `s` was moved. But if the async block just borrows `s`, it works fine.

In our case:

If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n// file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.\n\nuse reqwest::header::{HeaderMap, HeaderValue, CONTENT_TYPE};\nuse reqwest::{Client, RequestBuilder, Response};\nuse serde::de::DeserializeOwned;\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse tokio::sync::Mutex;\nuse url::form_urlencoded::Serializer as UrlSerializer;\n\nuse crate::core::config::ResolvedConfig;\nuse crate::core::constants::DEFAULT_PAGINATION_LIMIT;\nuse crate::core::errors::{ErrorCategory, ErrorContext, RedmineError};\nuse crate::http::pagination::PaginationParams;\nuse crate::http::rate_limiter::SlidingWindow;\n\nconst USER_AGENT: &str = concat!(\"redmine-wrapper-rs/\", env!(\"CARGO_PKG_VERSION\"));\n\n/// Cliente HTTP interno que gerencia autenticação, rate limiting e parsing.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub(crate) struct HttpClient {\n    client: Client,\n    config: ResolvedConfig,\n    rate_limiter: Mutex<SlidingWindow>,\n}\n\nimpl HttpClient {\n    /// Cria um novo HttpClient a partir da config resolvida.\n    pub fn new(config: ResolvedConfig) -> Result<Self, RedmineError> {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        headers.insert(CONTENT_TYPE, HeaderValue::from_static(\"application/json\"));\n\n        let client = Client::builder()\n            .default_headers(headers)\n            .timeout(config.timeout)\n            .user_agent(USER_AGENT)\n            .build()\n            .map_err(|e| RedmineError::Config(format!(\"falha ao criar HTTP client: {e}\")))?;\n\n        Ok(Self {\n            client,\n            rate_limiter: Mutex::new(SlidingWindow::new(config.max_rps)),\n            config,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma requisição GET e desserializa a resposta JSON.\n    pub async fn get<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        query: &[(&str, String)],\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let url = self.build_url(path, query)?;\n        let builder = self\n            .client\n            .get(&url)\n            .headers(self.auth_headers());\n        self.execute(builder, operation).await\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma requisição POST com corpo JSON e desserializa a resposta.\n    pub async fn post<T: DeserializeOwned, B: Serialize>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        body: &B,\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let url = self.build_url(path, &[])?;\n        let json_body = serde_json::to_string(body)?;\n        let builder = self\n            .client\n            .post(&url)\n            .headers(self.auth_headers())\n            .body(json_body);\n        self.execute(builder, operation).await\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma requisição POST com dados binários (upload de arquivos).\n    pub async fn post_binary<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        data: &[u8],\n        content_type: &str,\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let url = self.build_url(path, &[])?;\n        let builder = self\n            .client\n            .post(&url)\n            .headers(self.auth_headers())\n            .header(CONTENT_TYPE, content_type)\n            .body(data.to_vec());\n        self.execute(builder, operation).await\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma requisição PUT com corpo JSON para atualizar um recurso.\n    pub async fn put<T: DeserializeOwned, B: Serialize>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        body: &B,\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let url = self.build_url(path, &[])?;\n        let json_body = serde_json::to_string(body)?;\n        let builder = self\n            .client\n            .put(&url)\n            .headers(self.auth_headers())\n            .body(json_body);\n        self.execute(builder, operation).await\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma GET e extrai um campo específico do JSON de resposta.\n    pub async fn get_single<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        key: &str,\n        query: &[(&str, String)],\n        op: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let v: serde_json::Value = self.get(path, query, op).await?;\n        let inner = v.get(key).ok_or_else(|| {\n            RedmineError::api(\n                ErrorCategory::ParseError,\n                200,\n                format!(\"campo '{key}' não encontrado\"),\n                ErrorContext {\n                    operation: Some(op.into()),\n                    ..Default::default()\n                },\n            )\n        })?;\n        serde_json::from_value(inner.clone()).map_err(RedmineError::from)\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma POST e extrai um campo específico do JSON de resposta.\n    pub async fn post_single<T: DeserializeOwned, B: Serialize>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        key: &str,\n        body: &B,\n        op: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let v: serde_json::Value = self.post(path, body, op).await?;\n        let inner = v.get(key).ok_or_else(|| {\n            RedmineError::api(\n                ErrorCategory::ParseError,\n                201,\n                format!(\"campo '{key}' não encontrado\"),\n                ErrorContext {\n                    operation: Some(op.into()),\n                    ..Default::default()\n                },\n            )\n        })?;\n        serde_json::from_value(inner.clone()).map_err(RedmineError::from)\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma GET com suporte a paginação e retorna os itens da página\n    /// juntamente com o total de registros disponíveis.\n    pub async fn get_paginated<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        item_key: &str,\n        params: Option<&PaginationParams>,\n        query: &[(&str, String)],\n        op: &str,\n    ) -> Result<(Vec<T>, u32), RedmineError> {\n        let mut q = query.to_vec();\n        if let Some(p) = params {\n            for (k, v) in p.to_query() {\n                q.push((k, v));\n            }\n        } else {\n            q.push((\"limit\", DEFAULT_PAGINATION_LIMIT.to_string()));\n        }\n        let v: serde_json::Value = self.get(path, &q, op).await?;\n        let total = v\n            .get(\"total_count\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n            .unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n        let items = v\n            .get(item_key)\n            .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n            .unwrap_or_default();\n        Ok((items, total))\n    }\n\n    /// Executa requisições GET paginadas automaticamente até obter todos os\n    /// registros.\n    pub async fn get_all_paginated<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        item_key: &str,\n        query: &[(&str, String)],\n        op: &str,\n    ) -> Result<Vec<T>, RedmineError> {\n        let limit = DEFAULT_PAGINATION_LIMIT;\n        let mut offset = 0u32;\n        let mut all = Vec::new();\n        loop {\n            let mut pq = query.to_vec();\n            pq.push((\"offset\", offset.to_string()));\n            pq.push((\"limit\", limit.to_string()));\n            let v: serde_json::Value = self.get(path, &pq, op).await?;\n            let total = v\n                .get(\"total_count\")\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_u64())\n                .unwrap_or(0) as u32;\n            let items: Vec<T> = v\n                .get(item_key)\n                .and_then(|v| serde_json::from_value(v.clone()).ok())\n                .unwrap_or_default();\n            let c = items.len() as u32;\n            all.extend(items);\n            if offset + c >= total {\n                break;\n            }\n            offset += limit;\n        }\n        Ok(all)\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma requisição DELETE para remover um recurso.\n    pub async fn delete(\n        &self,\n        path: &str,\n        query: &[(&str, String)],\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<(), RedmineError> {\n        let url = self.build_url(path, query)?;\n        let builder = self\n            .client\n            .delete(&url)\n            .headers(self.auth_headers());\n        self.execute_void(builder, operation).await\n    }\n\n    fn auth_headers(&self) -> HeaderMap {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        if let Some(ref token) = self.config.token {\n            headers.insert(\n                \"X-Redmine-API-Key\",\n                HeaderValue::from_str(token).unwrap(),\n            );\n        }\n        if let Some(ref switch_user) = self.config.switch_user {\n            headers.insert(\n                \"X-Redmine-Switch-User\",\n                HeaderValue::from_str(switch_user).unwrap(),\n            );\n        }\n        headers\n    }\n\n    fn build_url(&self, path: &str, query: &[(&str, String)]) -> Result<String, RedmineError> {\n        let mut url = self.config.api_url(path);\n        if !query.is_empty() {\n            url.push('?');\n            let mut serializer = UrlSerializer::new(&mut url);\n            for (k, v) in query {\n                serializer.append_pair(k, v);\n            }\n            serializer.finish();\n        }\n        Ok(url)\n    }\n\n    async fn acquire_rate_limit(&self) {\n        let mut limiter = self.rate_limiter.lock().await;\n        limiter.acquire().await;\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma requisição, aplica rate limiting e parseia a resposta JSON.\n    async fn execute<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        &self,\n        builder: RequestBuilder,\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        self.acquire_rate_limit().await;\n\n        let response = builder.send().await.map_err(|e| {\n            if e.is_timeout() {\n                RedmineError::Timeout {\n                    duration: self.config.timeout,\n                    context: Box::new(ErrorContext {\n                        operation: Some(operation.into()),\n                        ..Default::default()\n                    }),\n                }\n            } else {\n                RedmineError::Http(e)\n            }\n        })?;\n\n        Self::handle_response(response, operation).await\n    }\n\n    /// Executa uma requisição, aplica rate limiting e descarta o corpo da resposta.\n    async fn execute_void(\n        &self,\n        builder: RequestBuilder,\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<(), RedmineError> {\n        self.acquire_rate_limit().await;\n\n        let response = builder.send().await.map_err(|e| {\n            if e.is_timeout() {\n                RedmineError::Timeout {\n                    duration: self.config.timeout,\n                    context: Box::new(ErrorContext {\n                        operation: Some(operation.into()),\n                        ..Default::default()\n                    }),\n                }\n            } else {\n                RedmineError::Http(e)\n            }\n        })?;\n\n        let status = response.status();\n        if status.is_success() {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        let body = response.text().await.unwrap_or_default();\n        Err(Self::map_error(status.as_u16(), &body, operation))\n    }\n\n    async fn handle_response<T: DeserializeOwned>(\n        response: Response,\n        operation: &str,\n    ) -> Result<T, RedmineError> {\n        let status = response.status();\n\n        if status == 204 {\n            return Err(RedmineError::api(\n                ErrorCategory::ParseError,\n                204,\n                \"resposta 204 sem corpo\",\n                ErrorContext {\n                    operation: Some(operation.into()),\n                    http_status: Some(204),\n                    ..Default::default()\n                },\n            ));\n        }\n\n        let body = response.text().await.unwrap_or_default();\n\n        if !status.is_success() {\n            return Err(Self::map_error(status.as_u16(), &body, operation));\n        }\n\n        serde_json::from_str(&body).map_err(|e| {\n            tracing::error!(\n                target: \"redmine_wrapper::http\",\n                \"{}: falha ao desserializar resposta: {}\",\n                operation,\n                e,\n            );\n            let ctx = ErrorContext {\n                operation: Some(operation.into()),\n                http_status: Some(status.as_u16()),\n                response_body: Some(body),\n                ..Default::default()\n            };\n            RedmineError::api(ErrorCategory::ParseError, status.as_u16(), e.to_string(), ctx)\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn map_error(status: u16, body: &str, operation: &str) -> RedmineError {\n        let category = ErrorCategory::from_status(status).unwrap_or(ErrorCategory::InternalError);\n\n        let api_errors: Option<Vec<String>> = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(body)\n            .ok()\n            .and_then(|v| {\n                v.get(\"errors\")\n                    .and_then(|e| serde_json::from_value(e.clone()).ok())\n            });\n\n        let detail = api_errors\n            .as_ref()\n            .and_then(|e| e.first().cloned())\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| match category {\n                ErrorCategory::RateLimited => \"limite de taxa excedido\".into(),\n                ErrorCategory::ImpersonationFailed => \"impersonação inválida\".into(),\n                _ => format!(\"erro HTTP {status}\"),\n            });\n\n        let context = ErrorContext {\n            operation: Some(operation.into()),\n            http_status: Some(status),\n            api_errors,\n            response_body: Some(body.into()),\n            ..Default::default()\n        };\n\n        if category == ErrorCategory::RateLimited {\n            let retry_after = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(body)\n                .ok()\n                .and_then(|v| v.get(\"retry_after\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()));\n            return RedmineError::RateLimited {\n                retry_after,\n                context: Box::new(context),\n            };\n        }\n\n        RedmineError::api(category, status, detail, context)\n    }\n}\n"
}

💭 **Reasoning:**

<details>
<summary>Click to expand reasoning</summary>

Let me use a script to convert the resources. The pattern is:
1. `pub fn` (with `#[must_use]` before it) → `pub async fn` (remove `#[must_use]`)
2. `.http.get_single(` → `.http.get_single(` + `.await`
3. `.http.get_all_paginated(` → `.http.get_all_paginated(` + `.await`
4. `.http.post_single(` → `.http.post_single(` + `.await`
5. `.http.post(` → `.http.post(` + `.await`
6. `.http.put(` → `.http.put(` + `.await`
7. `.http.delete(` → `.http.delete(` + `.await`
8. `.http.get_paginated(` → `.http.get_paginated(` + `.await`
9. `.http.get(` → `.http.get(` + `.await`
10. `.http.post_binary(` → `.http.post_binary(` + `.await`

Let me use `sed` to do all these replacements at once.

</details>

#### 🔧 Tool: bash
**Status:** completed
**Title:** for f in /projects/my/redmine-wrapper-rs/src/resources/*.rs; do
    if [ "$(basename $f)" = "mod.rs" ]; then continue; fi
    echo "Processing: $f"
    
    # Remove #[must_use] lines
    sed -i '/#\[must_use\]/d' "$f"
    
    # Convert pub fn to pub async fn
    sed -i 's/^    pub fn /    pub async fn /' "$f"
    
    # Add .await to http method calls (but not already awaited ones)
    sed -i 's/self\.http\.get_single(/self.http.get_single(/g' "$f"
    sed -i 's/self\.http\.get_all_paginated(/self.http.get_all_paginated(/g' "$f"
    sed -i 's/self\.http\.post_single(/self.http.post_single(/g' "$f"
    sed -i 's/self\.http\.post_binary(/self.http.post_binary(/g' "$f"
    sed -i 's/self\.http\.get_paginated(/self.http.get_paginated(/g' "$f
    sed -i 's/self\.http\.delete(/self.http.delete(/g' "$f
    
    # Now add .await to all http calls that end a statement
    # http calls ending at ; or ? 
    sed -i 's/self\.http\.\([a-z_]*\)(\(.*\));$/\0\nCHECK: self.http.\1(\2).await;/' "$f"
done
echo "Done"
